questions.text: Where is David? ____ is at home.

explanation.es: El pronombre en función de sujeto sustituye el sujeto, que en este ejemplo es David. Este pronombre en inglés, a diferencia del español, debe figurar siempre.
explanation.en: The subject pronoun is used to substitute the subject , which in this example is David. In English the subject pronoun is mandatory.

questions.text: Can you help ____?

explanation.es: Los pronombre que funcionan como objeto se colocan detrás del verbo al que complementan.
explanation.en: Object pronouns function as the object of a verb and are located after the verb that they complement.

questions.text: Where is the book? ____ is on the table.

explanation.es: La forma neutra "it" se utiliza para referirnos a cosas, animales (de los que no sepamos el sexo), así como a la hora y el clima.
explanation.en: The neuter form is used to refer to things, animals (for which we do not know the sex), as well as the time and the weather.

questions.text: ____ house is blue.

explanation.es: Los adjetivos posesivos siempre van antes del objeto.
explanation.en: Possessive adjectives are always found before the object.

questions.text: These seats are ____.

explanation.es: Los pronombres posesivos funcionan como el complemento directo y siempre va al final de la oración.
explanation.en: Possessive pronouns act as a direct complement and are therefore found at the end of the sentence.

questions.text: Whose bike is it? It is ____.

explanation.es: El genitivo (la terminación "-s") indica posesión y se utiliza cuando el orador quiere conocer el nombre del poseedor.
explanation.en: The genitive ("-s" termination) indicates possession and is used when the speaker wants to acknowledge the name of the possessor.

questions.text: I like ____ book.

explanation.es: Los pronombres demostrativos pueden estar en singular o plural y que pueden hacer referencia a la distancia. "This" (singular y aquí).
explanation.en: Demonstrative pronouns can be singular or plural and they make reference to distance. "This" (singular and here).

questions.text: ____ seats are ours.

explanation.es: Los pronombres demostrativos pueden estar en singular o plural y que pueden hacer referencia a la distancia. "Those" (plural y allí).
explanation.en: Demonstrative pronouns can be singular or plural and they make reference to distance. "Those" (plural and there).

questions.text: Did you paint the house?  Yes, we painted the house ____.

explanation.es: Los pronombres reflexivos se usan cuando el sujeto y el complemento del verbo son lo mismo.
explanation.en: Reflexive pronouns are used when the subject and complement of the verb are the same.

questions.text: Alice and Paul see ____ everyday.

explanation.es: Los pronombres recíprocos se forman con la frase "each other". En contraste con el reflexivo, tenemos dos personas diferentes que hacen una acción una a la otra.
explanation.en: Reciprocal pronouns are formed with the phrase “each other”. In contrast to the reflexive, we have two different subjects that do an action one to the other.

questions.text: I don't see them. Do _____ see them?

explanation.en: The subject of a sentence is the the person or thing that performs the action of the verb. In English, the subject pronoun is mandatory.

questions.text: We are going to the cinema. Do you want to come with _____?

questions.text: I love this book! Did you read _____?

explanation.en: The subject pronoun is used to substitute the subject , which in this example is "the cat". In English the subject pronoun is mandatory.

questions.text: It's Mary's birthday today. I called _____ this morning.

instruction.es: Elige el pronombre reflexivo correcto para completar la frase: myself / yourself / yourselves / himself / herself / itself / ourselves / themselves / each other
instruction.en: Choose the correct reflexive pronoun to complete the sentences: myself / yourself / yourselves / himself / herself / itself / ourselves / themselves / each other

questions.text: You and Sam can do the exercise _____.

explanation.es: Los pronombres reflexivos se usan cuando el sujeto y el complemento del verbo son lo mismo. "You" puede ser singular o plural.
explanation.en: Reflexive pronouns are used when the subject and complement of the verb are the same. "You" can be either singular or plural.

questions.text: Kim kissed Tom. Tom kissed Kim. They kissed _____.

explanation.es: Los pronombres recíprocos se forman con las partículas "each other". Esta forma no es reflexiva sino es una forma recíproca. A diferencia del reflexivo, tenemos dos sujetos diferentes que hablan el uno al otro.
explanation.en: Reciprocal pronouns are formed with the phrase "each other". This is not a reflexive form, but rather a reciprocal one. In contrast to the reflexive, we have two different subjects that are talking one to one.

questions.text: He did his homework _____.

explanation.es: Los pronombres reflexivos se usan cuando el sujeto y el complemento del verbo son lo mismo. "You" puede ser singular o plural.
explanation.en: Reflexive pronouns are used when the subject and the complement of the verb are the same.

questions.text: We can make dinner _____.

explanation.es: Los pronombres reflexivos se usan cuando el sujeto y el complemento del verbo son lo mismo.
explanation.en: Reflexive pronouns are used when the subject and the complement of the verb are the same.

questions.text: The dog just hurt _____ on that rock.

explanation.es: Los pronombres reflexivos se usan cuando el sujeto y el complemento del verbo son lo mismo.
explanation.en: Reflexive pronouns are used when the subject and the complement of the verb are the same.

questions.text: She taught _____ to play the piano.

explanation.es: Los pronombres reflexivos se usan cuando el sujeto y el complemento del verbo son lo mismo.
explanation.en: Reflexive pronouns are used when the subject and the complement of the verb are the same.

questions.text: That dog just bit ____.

instruction.es: Elige la respuesta correcta.
instruction.en: Choose the correct answer.

questions.text: What is ____ name of the hotel?

explanation.es: Usamos el artículo determinado cuando sabemos de quién o de qué estamos hablando.
explanation.en: We use "the" when we know who or what we are talking about.

questions.text: She works in ____ hospital.

explanation.es: "A" se utiliza con nombres que comienzan por consonante. Se usa para hablar de algo en general.
explanation.en: "A" is used with nouns that begin with a consonant. It is used to speak of something in general.

questions.text: I like to eat ____ apples.

explanation.es: No usamos un artículo cuando hablamos de algo en general.
explanation.en: We do not use an article when we are speaking of something in general.

questions.text: New York is in ____ United States.

explanation.es: Se usa el artículo determinado con los nombres de regiones geológicas, cadenas de montañas, mares, océanos, grupos de islas, ríos y países en plural.
explanation.en: We use the definite article with geological regions, mountain chains, seas, oceans, island groups, rivers and countries in plural.

questions.text: I am ____ republican.

explanation.es: Se usa el artículo indeterminado para hablar del oficio de alguien o de afiliación política.
explanation.en: With professions, offices and political affiliations, we use the indefinite article.

questions.text: We go to the supermarket on ____ Saturdays.

explanation.es: Nunca utilizaremos "the" cuando nos referimos a la television, o cuando nos referimos a las horas de las comidas, los días de la semana, los meses del año, las estaciones, los años o la hora.
explanation.en: We never use "the" when we are referring to television, meals, days of the week, months of the year, seasons, years or the hour.

questions.text: He has ____ red bike.

explanation.es: Se utiliza el artículo indeterminado para hablar de algo en general.
explanation.en: We use the indefinite article when we are speaking of something in general.

questions.text: I want ____ orange and two apples.

explanation.es: "An" se usa con nombres que comienzan por vocal. Se puede usar el artículo indeterminado en vez de "1".
explanation.en: We use "an" for nouns that begin with a vowel. The indefinite article can be used in place of "1".

questions.text: Her father is ____ architect.

explanation.es: "An" se usa con nombres que comienzan por vocal. Se usa el artículo indeterminado para hablar del oficio de alguien o de afiliación política.
explanation.en: "An" is used with nouns that begin with a vowel. With professions, offices and political affiliations, we use the indefinite article.

questions.text: We live in _____ house.  _____ house is small.

explanation.es: Se usa el artículo indeterminado para referirnos a una cosa por primera vez. La próxima vez que nos refiramos a esta cosa, utilizaremos el artículo determinado.
explanation.en: We use "a" or "an" when we make reference to something for the first time; the next reference we make, we use the definite article.

instruction.es: Si el artículo determinado no es necesario, escriba "-".
instruction.en: If the definite article is not necessary, write "-".

questions.text: I like _____ one in _____ middle.

explanation.es: Utilizamos "the" para indicar algo o alguien en particular, por ello se llama definido.
explanation.en: We use "the" to indicate something or someone in particular, as such it is called the definite article.

questions.text: I go to _____ school every day.

explanation.es: No usamos el artículo determinado cuando hablamos de instituciones en general.
explanation.en: We don't use the definite article when we are speaking of institutions in general.

questions.text: They live in _____ United States

explanation.es: Utilizamos "the" con los nombres de regiones geológicas, cadenas de montañas, mares, océanos, grupos de islas, ríos y países en plural.
explanation.en: We use the definite article with geological regions, mountain chains, seas, oceans, island groups, rivers and countries in plural.

questions.text: Do you remember _____ hotel we loved in _____ New York?

explanation.es: Utilizamos "the" para indicar algo o alguien en particular. No se utiliza el artículo con nombres de ciudades.
explanation.en: We use "the" to indicate something or someone in particular. "The" is not used with city names or places in general.

questions.text: The children watch _____ TV after school.

explanation.es: Nunca utilizaremos "the" cuando nos referimos a la televisión.
explanation.en: We never use "the" when we are referring to television.

questions.text: Do you like _____ coffee?

explanation.es: No usamos el artículo determinado cuando hablamos de algo en general.
explanation.en: We don't use the definite article when we are speaking of something in general.

instruction.es: Elige la respuesta correcta.
instruction.en: Choose the correct answer.

questions.text: That is ____ restaurant I like best.

explanation.es: Utilizamos "the" cuando sabemos de quién o de qué estamos hablando; para indicar algo o alguien en particular, por ello se llama el artículo determinado.
explanation.en: We use “the” when we know who or what we are talking about. We use “the” to indicate something or someone in particular, as such it is called the definite article.

questions.text: This is ____ Pacific Ocean.

explanation.es: Usamos el artículo determinado con los nombres de regiones geológicas, cadenas de montañas, mares, océanos, grupos de islas, ríos y países en plural.
explanation.en: We use the definite article with geological regions, mountain chains, seas, oceans, island groups, rivers and countries in plural.

questions.text: Put your name at ____ top of the page please.

explanation.es: Utilizamos "the" para hacer referencia a direcciones* (right, left, top, bottom) *y a los puntos cardinales* (north, south, east, west).
explanation.en: We use "the" to make reference to directions (right, left, top, bottom) or the cardinal points (north, south, east, west).

questions.text: He goes to ____ work everyday at 8 o'clock.

explanation.es: Con instituciones y modos de transporte, cuando estamos hablando en general, no utilizamos el artículo deterrminado.
explanation.en: With institutions and modes of transportation, when we are speaking in general, we do not use the definite article.

questions.text: My parents watch ____ television every night after dinner.

explanation.es: Nunca utilizaremos "the" cuando nos referimos a la televisión, o cuando nos referimos a las horas de las comidas, los días de la semana, los meses del año, las estaciones, los años o la hora.
explanation.en: We never use "the" when we are referring to television, meals, days of the week, months of the year, seasons, years or the hour.

questions.text: I love ____ chocolate.

explanation.es: No usamos el artículo determinado cuando hablamos de algo en general.
explanation.en: We do not use the definite article when we are speaking of something in general.

questions.text: She is ____ tallest girl in the class.

explanation.es: Utilizamos el artículo "the" con los adjetivos en grado superlativo y números ordinales.
explanation.en: With superlative adjectives and ordinal numbers we use the definite article.

questions.text: I go to ____ school by bus.

explanation.es: Con instituciones y modos de transporte, cuando estamos hablando en general, no utilizamos el artículo deterrminado.
explanation.en: With institutions and modes of transportation, when we are speaking in general, we do not use the definite article.

instruction.en: Complete the sentence with "a" or "an".

questions.text: I will have _____ cup of tea.

explanation.es: "A" se utiliza con nombres que comienzan por consonante.
explanation.en: "A" is used with nouns that begin with a consonant.

questions.text: He goes to _____ university in California.

explanation.es: Usamos "a" antes de las palabras que comienzan por la letras "u" o "eu" cuando estas son pronunciadas como el sonido figurado "yu".
explanation.en: We use "a" before words that begin with either "u" or "eu" when these are pronounced as "yu".

questions.text: Can I eat _____ apple?

explanation.es: "An" se usa con nombres que comienzan por vocal.
explanation.en: We add "-n" to the article for nouns that begin with a vowel.

questions.text: There is _____ excellent film at the cinema.

explanation.es: "An" se usa con nombres y adjetivos que comienzan por vocal.
explanation.en: We add "-n" to the article for nouns or adjectives that begin with a vowel.

questions.text: Can you give me _____ example?

explanation.es: "An" se usa con nombres que comienzan por vocal.
explanation.en: We add "-n" to the article for nouns that begin with a vowel.

questions.text: The train will arrive in _____ hour.

explanation.es: "An" se usa con palabras que comienzan por "h", pero sólo cuando esta no se pronuncia.
explanation.en: "An" is used with words that begin with "h", but only when it is not pronounced.

instruction.es: Elige la respuesta correcta.
instruction.en: Choose the correct answer.

questions.text: My father is ____ doctor.

explanation.es: Se usa el artículo indeterminado para hablar del oficio de alguien o de afiliación política.
explanation.en: With professions, offices and political affiliations, we use the indefinite article.

questions.text: My mother is ____ architect.

explanation.es: Se usa el artículo indeterminado para hablar del oficio de alguien o de afiliación política.
explanation.en: With professions, offices and political affiliations, we use the indefinite article.

questions.text: My sister goes to ____ university in the north.

explanation.es: Usamos “a” antes de las palabras que comienzan por la letras “u” o “eu” cuando estas son pronunciadas como el sonido figurado “yu”.
explanation.en: We use “a” before words that begin with either “u” or “eu” when these are pronounced as “yu”.

questions.text: There is ____ hotel near here.

explanation.es: Se utiliza el artículo indeterminado para hablar de algo en general.
explanation.en: We use the indefinite article when we are speaking of something in general.

questions.text: He is ____ honest person.

explanation.es: "An" se usa con palabras que comienzan por "*h*" cuando esta no se pronuncia.
explanation.en: "An" is used with words that begin with "h", but only when it is not pronounced.

questions.text: Have you got ____ umbrella?

explanation.es: "An" se usa con nombres que comienzan por vocal.
explanation.en: "An” is used for nouns that begin with a vowel.

questions.text: Can I have ____ apple?

explanation.es: "An" se usa con nombres que comienzan por vocal.
explanation.en: "An” is used for nouns that begin with a vowel.

questions.text: I have ____ green apple.

explanation.es: Puedes usar "a" o "an" en vez de “1”.
explanation.en: We can use “a” or “an” in place of “one” (singular).

instruction.es: Elige la respuesta correcta.
instruction.en: Choose the correct answer.

questions.text: New York is the largest city ____ the United States.

explanation.es: Se usa "in" para indicar que algo está dentro de una cosa, en un lugar cerrado, o en el interior de algo físicamente.
explanation.en: “In” is used to indicate that something is included within the limits of something, a closed space or the interior of something in a physical sense.

questions.text: The train is ____ the station.

explanation.es: Se usa "at" delante de edificios como casas, aeropuertos, universidades y para indicar acontecimientos como reuniones, fiestas, conciertos, deportes, etc.
explanation.en: “At” is used in front of buildings such as home, the airport, university, etc. to indicate that we are inside. It is used as well as to indicate events such as meetings, parties, concerts, sports events, etc.

questions.text: Her birthday is _____ the 1st _____ January.

explanation.es: Cuando decimos el día y mes se usa "on" para el día y "of" delante del mes. En este ejemplo, el primero de enero.
explanation.en: When we say a day and month we use "on" with the day and "of" before the month,

questions.text: We will arrive ____ 15 minutes.

explanation.es: Utilizamos "in" con períodos de tiempo (en, dentro de).
explanation.en: “In” is used with periods of time (within).

questions.text: The shop is ____ the bank and the supermarket.

explanation.es: "Between" indica que alguien o algo está en el espacio que separa dos otras personas o cosas.
explanation.en: "Between" indicates that someone or something is in the space that separates two other people or things.

questions.text: The page number is ____ the top of the page.

explanation.es: Se usa "at" delante de "the top" or "the bottom".
explanation.en: "At" is used before "the top" or "the bottom".

questions.text: They lived _____ China _____ three years.

explanation.es: Se usa "in" para indicar lugares geográficas y "for" para la duración de tiempo.
explanation.en: "In" is used for geographical places and "for" indicates a duration of time.

questions.text: I will see you ____ the morning.

explanation.es: Utilizamos "in" con partes del día.
explanation.en: “In” is used with parts of the day.

questions.text: What did Jenny say ____?

explanation.es: Se usa "to" con el verbo "say" y un objeto personal.
explanation.en: "To" is used with the verb "say" and a direct object.

questions.text: The dog jumped ____ the wall.

explanation.es: Se usa "over" para indicar un movimiento sobre o encima de algo.
explanation.en: "Over" is used to express in an upward and forward direction.

instruction.es: Completa la frase con: in / at / on

questions.text: My birthday is _____  June.

explanation.es: Utilizamos "in" con meses, años, épocas, partes del día y períodos de tiempo.
explanation.en: "In" is used with months, years, periods of time, seasons and parts of the day.

questions.text: We leave for Paris _____ Monday.

explanation.es: Utilizamos "on" con días de la semana, fechas y fiestas.
explanation.en: We use "on" with days of the week, dates and holidays.

questions.text: My phone is not _____ my bag.

explanation.es: Utilizamos "in" para indicar que algo está dentro de una cosa, en un lugar cerrado, o en el interior de algo físicamente.
explanation.en: We use "in" to indicate that something is included within the limits of something, a closed space or the interior of something in a physical sense.

questions.text: He was born _____ 1990.

explanation.es: Utilizamos "in" con meses, años, épocas, partes del día y períodos de tiempo.
explanation.en: "In" is used with months, years, periods of time, seasons and parts of the day.

questions.text: Please sign _____ the bottom of this page.

explanation.es: Se usa "at" delante de edificios, para acontecimientos como reuniones, fiestas, conciertos, deportes, etc., antes de "top" o "bottom".
explanation.en: "At" is used in front of buildings and before "top" and "bottom", as well as to indicate events such as meetings, parties, concerts, sports events, etc.

questions.text: The conference starts _____ January 12th.

explanation.es: Utilizamos "on" con días de la semana, fechas y fiestas.
explanation.en: We use "on" with days of the week, dates and holidays.

instruction.es: Elige la respuesta correcta.
instruction.en: Choose the correct answer.

questions.text: There are many tourists ____ New York.

explanation.es: Se usa "in" para indicar tanto espacios cerrados como espacios abiertos. Lo utilizamos para indicar que algo está dentro de una cosa, en un lugar cerrado, o en el interior de algo físicamente.
explanation.en: “In” is used to indicate both open and closed spaces. We use this preposition to indicate that something is included within the limits of something, a closed space or the interior of something in a physical sense.

questions.text: I met him ____ the South of Spain.

explanation.es: Se utiliza "in" para indicar que se está en un lugar geográfico.
explanation.en: “In” is used to indicate the geographical location of something.

questions.text: My apartment is ____ the first floor.

explanation.es: Se coloca "on" delante de nombres de lugares con base como mesas, suelos, etc…, cuando nos referimos a lugares de una habitación como techo o pared y para indicar que alguien está dentro de un transporte público o dentro de una planta de un edificio.
explanation.en: “On” is used with surfaces, when we are referencing a location within a room, such as the ceiling or the wall, or to indicate that someone is inside a mode of transport or on the floor of a building.

questions.text: The meeting is ____ 3:30.

explanation.es: Utilizamos "at" delante de las horas.
explanation.en: We use “at” before the hour or holidays.

questions.text: I'm going to Mexico City ____ this weekend.

explanation.es: No hace falta una preposición en este ejemplo porque "this" indica el tiempo especifico. Podrías decir en este caso "at the weekend".
explanation.en: A preposition is not necessary here, as "this" indicates a specific time.

questions.text: We often go to the beach ____ the summer.

explanation.es: Utilizamos "in" con épocas y estaciones.
explanation.en: "In" is used as a preposition of time with months, years, periods of time, seasons and parts of the day.

questions.text: My birthday is ____ the 10th of June.

explanation.es: Utilizamos "on" con fechas.
explanation.en: “On” is used with days of the week, dates and holidays.

questions.text: His birthday is ____ May.

explanation.es: Utilizamos "in" con meses.
explanation.en: “In” is used with months, years, periods of time (duration), seasons and parts of the day.

questions.text: Every year I go home ____ Christmas.

explanation.es: Utilizamos "at" delante de fiestas.
explanation.en: We use “at” before the hour or holidays.

questions.text: We are getting married ____ the spring.

explanation.es: Utilizamos "in" con épocas o estaciones.
explanation.en: “In” is used with months, years, periods of time (duration), seasons and parts of the day.

instruction.es: Elige una de las palabras para completar la frase: next to / by / between / behind / in front of / opposite / under / above / below
instruction.en: Choose one of the following words to complete the sentence: next to / by / between / behind / in front of / opposite / under / above / below

questions.text: I am in front of you. You are _____ me.

explanation.es: "Behind" es el opuesto de "in front of".
explanation.en: "Behind" is the opposite of "in front of".

questions.text: Come and sit _____ me.

explanation.es: Tanto "next to" como "beside" se pueden utilizar indistintamente.
explanation.en: "Next to" and "beside" can be used interchangeably.

questions.text: I am sitting at the table. My feet are _____ the table.
There is a painting on the wall _____ the sofa.
[Jim, Susie, John] Susie is _____ Jim and John.
We are having a conversation face to face. We are standing _____ each other.

explanation.es: "Opposite" significa delante y cara a cara, en cambio "in front of" significa delante de pero no cara a cara.
explanation.en: "Opposite" indicates that two people are facing one another (face to face), while "in front of" implies that one person is ahead of the other with their back to the person behind, as in a line or queue.

instruction.es: Elige la respuesta correcta.
instruction.en: Choose the correct answer.

questions.text: The table is below the window. The window is ____ the table.

explanation.es: "Above" se utiliza para hacer comparaciones. Implica que algo está más arriba que otra cosa.
explanation.en: "Above" is used for making comparisons. It implies that something is higher than something else.

questions.text: Steve, Alex, David. Alex is ____ Steve and David.

explanation.es: "Between" indica que alguien o algo está en el espacio que separa dos otras personas o cosas.
explanation.en: "Between" indicates that someone or something is in the space that separates two other people or things.

questions.text: Steve, Alex, David. Alex is ____ Steve.

explanation.es: "Next to" significa al lado de o junto a.
explanation.en: "Next to" means adjacent or by the side of.

questions.text: Brenda is in front of Gina. Gina is ____ Brenda.

explanation.es: "Behind" aquí indica que Gina está detrás de Brenda. Es lo contrario de "delante de".
explanation.en: "Behind" here indicates that Gina is in back of Brenda. It is the opposite of "in front of".

questions.text: Your mouth is ____ your nose.

explanation.es: "Under" indica que algo está por debajo de otra cosa.
explanation.en: "Under" indicates that something is below or beneath something else.

instruction.es: Elige la preposición de tiempo correcta para completar la frase: before / after / during / for
instruction.en: Choose the correct preposition of time to complete the sentence: before / after / during / for

questions.text: Please don't speak _____ the presentation.

explanation.es: "During" puede ir seguido de verbos y nombres o sustantivos.
explanation.en: "During" means "throughout the duration of or at a point in the course of".

questions.text: The film ends at 6 o'clock. I will see you at 6:30. I will see you _____ the film.

explanation.es: Se coloca "after" detrás de verbos y nombres o sustantivos.
explanation.en: "After" means "subsequent to in time".

questions.text: He lived in that house _____ 20 years.

explanation.es: Se coloca detrás de verbos y nombres o sustantivos. "For" se usa para expresar un período de tiempo ya sean días, horas, meses o años.
explanation.en: "For" is used to express a period of time such as hours, days, months, years, etc.

questions.text: You need to brush your teeth _____ going to bed.

explanation.es: Se coloca "before" detrás de verbos y nombres o sustantivos.
explanation.en: "Before" means "preceding, earlier than".

questions.text: We eat chocolate cake _____ dinner.

explanation.es: Se coloca "after" detrás de verbos y nombres o sustantivos.
explanation.en: "After" means "subsequent to in time".

questions.text: They traveled _____ 6 weeks.

explanation.es: "For" indica duración o un período de tiempo como horas, días, meses, años, etc.
explanation.en: "For" is used to express a period of time such as hours, days, months, years, etc.

instruction.es: Elige la respuesta correcta.
instruction.en: Choose the correct answer.

questions.text: Train A leaves at 11:00. Train B leaves at 12:00. The B train leaves ____ the A train.

explanation.es: "After" significa "después".
explanation.en: "After" means subsequent in time.

questions.text: Bus 1 arrives at 7:00. Bus 2 arrives at 5:00. Bus 2 arrives ____ Bus 1.

explanation.es: "Before" significa "antes"; indica que algo había sucedido antes de otra cosa.
explanation.en: "Before" indicates that something happened earlier than or preceded something else.

questions.text: She worked at the hotel ____ 5 years.

explanation.es: "For" indica la duración del tiempo; en cuánto tiempo ha pasado algo.
explanation.en: "For" is a function word used to indicate the duration of time; how long something has happened.

questions.text: The rain started ____ the football match. They had to stop the game.

explanation.es: "During" indica algo que sucedió entremedio de algo o durante.
explanation.en: "During" indicates something that happened throughout the duration of something else.

questions.text: He goes ____ disco techs every night.

explanation.es: "To" es la preposición de movimiento o dirección más común y se utiliza para mostrar movimiento en una dirección específica.
explanation.en: "To" is the most common preposition of direction or movement and is used to suggest movement in the direction of a person, place or thing.

questions.text: You must walk ____ the bridge.

explanation.es: "Across" se utiliza para mostrar el movimiento de un lado hacia el lado opuesto.
explanation.en: "Across" is used to show movement from one side to the opposite side.

questions.text: Walk ____ the street and turn left.

explanation.es: "Down" se utiliza para indicar el movimiento de una posición superior a una posición más baja.
explanation.en: "Down" is used to indicate movement from a higher to a lower position.

questions.text: That woman going ____ the supermarket is my mother.

explanation.es: "Into" indica movimiento hacia el interior de un espacio cerrado.
explanation.en: "Into" indicates movement towards the inside of or to enter in an enclosed space.

questions.text: Sally is talking ____ Michael.

explanation.es: "To" es la preposición más comun de dirección o movimiento y en este caso indica la dirección de la conversación.
explanation.en: "To" is the most common preposition of direction or movement and in this case indicates the direction of the conversation.

questions.text: I am not: ____

explanation.en: Short forms combine the pronoun with a verb ("be" or "have got") or auxiliary verb ("do" or "have"). While negative short forms consist of combining the verb with the negative auxiliary, this is not possible with "am not".

instruction.es: Escribe las siguientes frases en su forma afirmativa, negativa o interrogativa.
instruction.en: Rewrite the following sentences in the affirmative, negative or the interrogative.

questions.text: Write the sentence in the negative: He has got a car.

explanation.es: Hay varias formas cortas para el negativo de "have got".
explanation.en: There are various short forms for the negative of "have got".

answer.raw: He has not got a car. | He hasn't got a car. | He's not got a car.
accepted: He has not got a car.
accepted_placeholders: He has not got a car.

questions.text: Write the sentence in the interrogative: You have two brothers.

explanation.es: Con "have", necesitamos el verbo auxiliar "do" para formar frases interrogativas.
explanation.en: With "have", we need the auxiliary verb "do" to form interrogative sentences.

questions.text: Write the sentence in the interrogative: You have got a dog.

explanation.es: En las frases interrogativas con "have got" simplemente debemos cambiar el orden del sujeto y verbo.
explanation.en: In interrogative sentences with "have got" we must simply change the order of the subject and verb.

questions.text: Write the sentence in the negative: She has a big house.

explanation.es: Para formar el negativo de "have", debemos utilizar el verbo auxiliar "do" así como el auxiliar negativo "no".
explanation.en: To form the negative of "have", we must use the auxiliary verb "do" as well as the negative auxiliary "not".

answer.raw: She doesn't have a big house. | She does not have a big house.
accepted: She doesn't have a big house.
She does not have a big house.
accepted_placeholders: She doesn't have a big house.
She does not have a big house.

questions.text: Write the sentence in the negative: We have a cat.

explanation.es: Para formar el negativo de "have", debemos utilizar el verbo auxiliar "do" así como el auxiliar negativo "no".
explanation.en: To form the negative of "have", we must use the auxiliary verb "do" as well as the negative auxiliary "not".

answer.raw: We do not have a cat. | We don't have a cat.
accepted: We do not have a cat.
accepted_placeholders: We do not have a cat.

questions.text: Write the sentence in the interrogative: They have dinner at 6 o'clock.

explanation.es: Con "have", necesitamos el verbo auxiliar "do" para formar frases interrogativas.
explanation.en: With "have", we need the auxiliary verb "do" to form interrogative sentences.

answer.raw: Do they have dinner at 6 o'clock?
accepted: Do they have dinner at 6 o'clock?
accepted_placeholders: Do they have dinner at 6 o'clock?

questions.text: Write the sentence in the affirmative: We haven't got a cat.

explanation.es: La afirmativa de "have" y "have got" no requieren el uso de un auxiliar.
explanation.en: The affirmative of "have" and "have got" do not require the use of an auxiliary.

answer.raw: We've got a cat. | We have got a cat.

questions.text: Write the sentence in the affirmative: Does he have a red bike?

explanation.es: La afirmativa de "have" y "have got" no requieren el uso de un auxiliar.
explanation.en: The affirmative of "have" and "have got" do not require the use of an auxiliary.

questions.text: Write the sentence in the interrogative: He hasn't got any pets.

explanation.es: En las frases interrogativas con "have got" simplemente debemos cambiar el orden del sujeto y verbo.
explanation.en: In interrogative sentences with "have got" we must simply change the order of the subject and verb.

instruction.es: Elige la respuesta correcta.
instruction.en: Choose the correct answer.

questions.text: ____ you help me?

explanation.es: En frases interrogativas, el uso de "can" puede solicitar permiso o preguntar sobre posibilidades.
explanation.en: In interrogative sentences, we use “can” to ask permission (informally) or to ask about possibilities.

questions.text: They ____ practice more if they want to win the championship.

explanation.es: "Should" indica un obligación o recomendación. Refleja una opinión sobre lo que es correcto.
explanation.en: Should indicates a recommendation or obligation or reflects an opinion about what is right or correct.

questions.text: You ____ not drink and drive.

explanation.es: "Must" indica una obligación, prohibición o necesidad.
explanation.en: “Must” indicates an obligation, prohibition (when in the negative) or necessity.

questions.text: ____ you always be late?

explanation.es: Es posible también usar "must" para preguntas retóricas.
explanation.en: “Must” can also be used in rhetorical questions.

questions.text: I ____ like to go to New York someday.

explanation.es: Se usa "would like" para declarar una preferencia.
explanation.en: "Would like" indicates preference, desire, or intention.

questions.text: They ____ to work less.

explanation.es: "Ought to" es un sinónimo de "should", indica un obligación o recomendación. Refleja un opinión sobre lo que es correcto y siempre está seguido por la preposición "to".
explanation.en: "Ought to" is a synonym of "should" and indicates an obligation or recommendation. It can reflect an opinion about what is right or correct and is always followed by the preposition "to".

questions.text: It ____ be dangerous to drive if you are tired.

explanation.es: "Can" indica posibilidad.
explanation.en: "Can" indicates possibility.

questions.text: ____ you help me move?

explanation.es: Se utiliza "will" en frases interrogativas para pedir información, un favor o sobre opciones.
explanation.en: “Will” is used in interrogative sentence to ask for information, a favor or opinion.

instruction.es: Elige la respuesta correcta.
instruction.en: Choose the correct answer.

questions.text: Megan ____ play both the piano and the guitar.

explanation.es: "Can" indica habilidad o posibilidad. En estos casos puede ser traducido como "poder" en español.
explanation.en: "Can" indicates ability or possibility.

questions.text: Start eating without me, I ____ be late.

explanation.es: Se usa "might" para indicar posibilidades en el presente o el futuro.
explanation.en: "Might" is used to indicate possibility in the present or future.

questions.text: You ____ wear a helmet when riding your bike.

explanation.es: "Must" indica una obligación, prohibición o necesidad.
explanation.en: "Must" indicates an obligation, prohibition (in the negative) or necessity.

questions.text: ____ you help me with my homework?

explanation.es: En frases interrogativas, el uso de "can" puede solicitar permiso o preguntar sobre posibilidades.
explanation.en: In interrogative sentences, we use "can" to ask permission (informally) or to ask about possibilities.

questions.text: I know it's difficult, but we ____ succeed!

explanation.es: El uso de "will" significa voluntad o determinación.
explanation.en: The use of "will" indicates willingness or determination.

questions.text: That cut looks infected, you ____ go to the doctor.

explanation.es: "Should" indica un obligación o recomendación. Refleja un opinión sobre lo que es correcto. Se traduce como el condicional de "deber" en español.
explanation.en: "Should" indicates a recommendation or obligation or reflects an opinion about what is right or correct.

questions.text: I ____ eat my dinner last night, my stomach hurt.

explanation.es: "Could" indica posibilidad o habilidad en el pasado.
explanation.en: "Could" is the past tense of "can" and indicates an ability or possibility in the past.

questions.text: Glen ____ come with us to the movies if he finishes his work.

explanation.es: Como "could", se usa "may" para indicar posibilidades en el futuro.
explanation.en: "May", like "could", is used to indicate possibilities in the future.

questions.text: I ____ like to learn English some day.

explanation.es: Se usa "would" para declarar una preferencia y para preguntar por algo educádamente.
explanation.en: We use "would" to indicate ad preference, desire or intention.

questions.text: We ____ break the window because we locked the keys inside the car.

explanation.es: "Have to" indica obligación, prohibición o necesidad como "must", pero no podemos usar "must" en el pasado.
explanation.en: "Have to", like "must", indicates an obligation, prohibition or necessity, but as we cannot use "must" in the past, we use "had to".

questions.text: I ____ in a bank.

explanation.es: Se utiliza el presente simple para hablar de generalidades o hechos científicos.
explanation.en: The present simple is used to speak about generalities or scientific facts.

questions.text: Barbara ____ everyday.

explanation.es: El presente simple se utiliza para hablar de cosas que suceden habitualmente.
explanation.en: We use the present simple when speaking about things that happen regularly.

questions.text: He ____ in Canada.

explanation.es: En frases negativas, el verbo auxiliar ("to do") cambia y el verbo principal es en el infinitivo.
explanation.en: In negative sentences with the auxiliary “to do”, it is the auxiliary verb that is conjugated in the third person, not the principal verb.

questions.text: We ____ English.

explanation.es: Usamos el presente continuo para hablar de algo que está sucediendo en la actualidad pero no necesariamente mientras hablamos.
explanation.en: We use the present continuous to talk about something that is happening now, but not necessarily at the moment when we are speaking.

questions.text: What are you doing right now? I ____ my homework.

explanation.es: El presente continuo se utiliza para hablar sobre algo que está pasando en el momento en el que hablamos.
explanation.en: The present continuous is used to speak about something that is happening at the moment in which we are speaking.

explanation.es: Usamos el presente continuo para hablar de algo que está ya decidido que se hará en el futuro próximo.
explanation.en: The present continuous is used to talk about something already decided in the near future.

questions.text: He ____ next week.

explanation.en: In negative sentences in the present continuous, the negative auxiliar (not) is found between the auxiliary verb (to be) and the gerund.

explanation.en: In interrogative sentences in the present continuous, the subject is found between the auxiliary verb (to be) and the gerund.

instruction.es: Escribe la forma de adverbio de los siguientes adjetivos.
instruction.en: Write the adverb form of the following adjectives.

questions.text: beautiful

explanation.es: La regla general para formar adverbios es añadir la terminación “-ly” a un adjetivo, que equivale a la terminación en castellano de (“-mente”). "Beautifully" significa "hermosamente".
explanation.en: The general rule for forming adverbs is to add the termination “-ly” to an adjective.

questions.text: horrible

explanation.es: Adjetivos terminados en “-le,” se cambia la terminación “-le” por “-ly.”
explanation.en: For adjectives that end in “-le”, the “e” is dropped and the termination “-ly” is added.

questions.text: fast

explanation.es: Algunos adverbios no terminan en “-ly.” El adjetivo y el adverbio son iguales.
explanation.en: Some adverbs don’t change. The adjective and adverb are the same.

questions.text: crazy

explanation.es: Para los adjetivos que terminan en consonante “-y,” cambian la terminación “-y” por la de “-ily.”
explanation.en: For those adjectives that end in “-y”, the “y” becomes an “i”. The termination becomes “-ily.”

questions.text: slow

explanation.es: La regla general para formar adverbios es añadir la terminación “-ly” a un adjetivo, que equivale a la terminación en castellano de “-mente”.
explanation.en: The general rule for forming adverbs is to add the termination “-ly” to an adjective.

questions.text: hard

explanation.es: Algunos adverbios no terminan en “-ly.” El adjetivo y el adverbio son iguales.

questions.text: careful

explanation.en: The general rule for forming adverbs is to add the termination “-ly” to an adjective.

questions.text: quiet

explanation.es: La regla general para formar adverbios es añadir la terminación “-ly” a un adjetivo, que equivale a la terminación en castellano de (“-mente”).
explanation.en: The general rule for forming adverbs is to add the termination “-ly” to an adjective.

questions.text: happy

explanation.es: Para los adjetivos que terminan en consonante “-y,” cambian la terminación “-y” por la de “-ily.”
explanation.en: For those adjectives that end in “-y”, the “y” becomes an “i”. The termination becomes “-ily.”

instruction.es: Escribe las palabras en el orden correcto para formar una frase.
instruction.en: Write the words in the correct order to form sentences.

questions.text: went / they / Rome / last month / to

explanation.es: Los adverbios de tiempo normalmente van al principio o al final de la oración.
explanation.en: Adverbs of time generally are located before the verb or at the end of the sentence.

answer.raw: They went to Rome last month. | Last month they went to Rome.
accepted: They went to Rome last month.
Last month they went to Rome.
accepted_placeholders: They went to Rome last month.
Last month they went to Rome.

questions.text: his / finished / already / dinner / he

explanation.es: "Already" normalmente va antes del verbo o al final de la oración.
explanation.en: "Already" is generally located before the verb or at the end of the sentence.

answer.raw: He already finished his dinner. | He finished his dinner already.

questions.text: yet / you / ? / are / ready

explanation.es: “Yet” siempre va al final de la oración.
explanation.en: “Yet” always goes at the end of the sentence.

questions.text: have / we / time / still

explanation.es: “Still” va delante del verbo, excepto con el verbo “to be”.
explanation.en: “Still” is located in front of the verb except when the verb is “to be”.

questions.text: happy / I / really / see / am / you / to

explanation.es: Los adverbios de grado van delante de la palabra que modifican.
explanation.en: Adverbs of degree are located in front of the word that they modify.

answer.raw: I am really happy to see you.
accepted: I am really happy to see you.
accepted_placeholders: I am really happy to see you.

questions.text: quickly / speak / very / you

explanation.es: Los adverbios de grado van delante de la palabra que modifican. Los adverbios de modo van detrás del objeto directo. Si no hay un objeto, van detrás del verbo.
explanation.en: Adverbs of degree are located in front of the word that they modify. Adverbs of manner are found after the direct object of the sentence or if there is no object, after the verb.

questions.text: never / eats / she / vegetables

explanation.es: Los adverbios de frecuencia van delante del verbo, a excepción de su uso con el verbo “to be” que se colocarán detrás.
explanation.en: Adverbs of frequency are located in front of the verb, unless the verb is "to be", in which case the adverb is located after the verb.

questions.text: they / late / class / always / are / to

explanation.es: Los adverbios de frecuencia van delante del verbo, a excepción de su uso con el verbo “to be” que se colocarán detrás.
explanation.en: Adverbs of frequency are located in front of the verb, unless the verb is "to be", in which case the adverb is located after the verb.

explanation.en: “Still” is located in front of the verb except when the verb is “to be”, in which case “still” is found after the verb.

questions.text: there / ? / yet / he / is

explanation.en: “Yet” always goes at the end of the sentence.

display.es: Test de unidad: Pronombres relativos y indefinidos
display.en: Unit Test: Relative and Indefinite Pronouns

instruction.es: Elige la respuesta correcta.
instruction.en: Choose the correct answer.

questions.text: I have a new job ____ pays very well.

explanation.es: "That" refers to a thing or a person.
explanation.en: "Where" refers to a place.

questions.text: This is the school ____ my sister works.

explanation.es: "Where" se refiere a un lugar.
explanation.en: "Where" refers to a place.

questions.text: There is ____ you can do.

explanation.es: Se usa "nothing" para expresar un concepto negativo "nada" pero SOLO con verbos afirmativos.
explanation.en: We use "nothing" to express a negative concept but ONLY with affirmative verbs.

questions.text: Brian, ____ is my best student, is going to study in England next year.

explanation.es: Usamos "who" para referirnos a personas en "non-defining realtive clauses".
explanation.en: We use "who"  to refer to people in non-defining relative clauses.

questions.text: May I have ____ glass of water?

explanation.es: Usamos "another"  delante de objetos para significar "otro" o "uno más".
explanation.en: We use "another" in front of objects to mean "an additional one".

questions.text: Is there ____ in the classroom?

explanation.es: Usamos "anybody" en preguntas.
explanation.en: We use "anybody" in questions.

questions.text: ____ is coming to the party.

explanation.es: "Everybody" y "everyone" significan "todos" o "todo el mundo".
explanation.en: "Everybody" and "everyone"  refers to all people.

instruction.es: Elige la respuesta correcta.
instruction.en: Choose the correct answer.

questions.text: There is ____ in the bathroom at the moment.

explanation.es: "Some" y sus compuestos como "somebody" se usan en frases afirmativas, y algunas preguntas.
explanation.en: "Some" and its compounds are used in affirmative sentences, and some questions.

questions.text: I am  bored, there isn't ____ to do here.

explanation.es: Los usos de los pronombres indefinidos compuestos con “some”, “any”, y “no” en frases afirmativas, negativas y interrogativas son los mismos de los usos de “some” y “any”.
explanation.en: The rules regulating the uses of the indefinite pronouns that are compounds of “some”, “any” and “no” are the same as the rules of use for these quantifiers (“some”, “any” and “no”) when used alone.

questions.text: I am bored, there is ____ to do here.

explanation.es: Los pronombres indefinidos en singular siempre llevan el verbo en singular.
explanation.en: The singular form of the verb (third person) is used with singular indefinite pronouns.

questions.text: I can't find my keys ____!

explanation.es: Los usos de los pronombres indefinidos compuestos con “some”, “any”, y “no” en frases afirmativas, negativas y interrogativas son los mismos de los usos de “some” y “any”.
explanation.en: The rules regulating the uses of the indefinite pronouns that are compounds of “some”, “any” and “no” are the same as the rules of use for these quantifiers (“some”, “any” and “no”) when used alone.

questions.text: ____ at the party had a great time.

explanation.es: Los pronombres indefinidos en singular siempre llevan el verbo en singular.
explanation.en: The singular form of the verb (third person) is used with singular indefinite pronouns.

questions.text: Is there ____ in that bag?

explanation.es: Usamos "anything" en preguntas y frases negativas. Significa "algo" en esta frase.
explanation.en: We use "anything" in questions and negative sentences.

questions.text: Are there _____ lemons? No, there are _____.

explanation.es: "Any" se usa con preguntas; "none" significa "ninguno".
explanation.en: "Any" is used in questions; "none" means "not any".

questions.text: I have two children. ____ play tennis.

explanation.es: "Both" se refiere a dos personas o cosas. Significa "ambos".
explanation.en: "Both" refers to two people or things.

instruction.es: Escribe las palabras en el orden correcto.
instruction.en: Write the words in the correct order.

questions.text: last / looked at / house / sold / that / the / we / is / week

explanation.es: El pronombre relativo "that" sólo se puede utilizar en cláusulas que definen.
explanation.en: The relative pronoun "that" can only be used in defining relative clauses.

answer.raw: The house that we looked at last week is sold.
accepted: The house that we looked at last week is sold.
accepted_placeholders: The house that we looked at last week is sold.

questions.text: Choose the correct sentence:

explanation.es: Las cláusulas que no definen añaden información adicional y se encuentran entre comas.
explanation.en: Non-defining relative clauses add information and are found between commas.

options.text: Peter, which is the manager, is not here today.
Peter, who is the manager, is not here today.
Peter who is the manager is not here today.
Peter which is the manager is not here today.

questions.text: ? / what is / of the hotel / the name / where you stayed

explanation.es: A veces utilizamos adverbios relativos como "where" en lugar de un pronombre relativo.
explanation.en: We sometimes use relative adverbs, such as "where", instead of a relative pronoun.

answer.raw: What is the name of the hotel where you stayed?
accepted: What is the name of the hotel where you stayed?
accepted_placeholders: What is the name of the hotel where you stayed?

questions.text: you / book / reading / I / are / the / like

explanation.es: Puede omitirse el pronombre relativo cuando es el objeto de la frase.
explanation.en: The relative pronoun is not necessary when it is used to refer to the object of the sentence.

answer.raw: I like the book you are reading.
accepted: I like the book you are reading.
accepted_placeholders: I like the book you are reading.

questions.text: Choose the correct sentence:

explanation.es: Las cláusulas que no definen añaden información adicional y se encuentran entre comas.
explanation.en: Non-defining relative clauses add information and are found between commas.

options.text: These shoes, that I bought for the party, aren't very comfortable.
These shoes that I bought for the party aren't very comfortable.
These shoes, which I bought for the party, aren't very comfortable.
These shoes, who I bought for the party, aren't very comfortable.

questions.text: ? / have / who / friend / do / us / a / you / help / can

explanation.es: A veces utilizamos adverbios relativos como "where" en lugar de un pronombre relativo.
explanation.en: We sometimes use relative adverbs, such as "where", instead of a relative pronoun.

answer.raw: Do you have a friend who can help us?
accepted: Do you have a friend who can help us?
accepted_placeholders: Do you have a friend who can help us?

explanation.en: The relative pronoun is not necessary when it is used to refer to the object of the sentence.

questions.text: Can you buy me the book that won the Booker Prize last year?

explanation.en: The relative pronoun is necessary unless it is referring to the object of the sentence.

questions.text: Bill has many CDs which were recorded by his brother's record company.

explanation.en: The relative pronoun is necessary unless it is referring to the object of the sentence.

questions.text: The man who I met at the party last week wants to go out to dinner this weekend.

explanation.es: Puede omitirse el pronombre relativo cuando es el objeto de la frase.

questions.text: Rachel said that she was going to Rome next week.

explanation.es: Puede omitirse el pronombre relativo cuando es el objeto de la frase.
explanation.en: The relative pronoun is not necessary when it is used to refer to the object of the sentence.

questions.text: My birthday, which is also the birthday of my father, is September 3rd.

explanation.es: Hay dos tipos de claúsulas relativas, las que definen al sustantivo (identifican a que cosa o persona nos referimos) y las que no lo definen ( sólo agregan información adicional). Se pone coma para separar la claúsula relativa del resto de la oración.
explanation.en: Non-defining relative clauses add additional information to a sentence and are always found between commas.

options.text: It is a defining relative clause.
It is a non-defining relative clause.

questions.text: I received many gifts for my birthday, but the gifts I love the most are the ones that my children gave me.

explanation.es: Hay dos tipos de claúsulas relativas, las que definen al sustantivo (identifican a que cosa o persona nos referimos) y las que no lo definen ( sólo agregan información adicional). Se pone coma para separar la claúsula relativa del resto de la oración.
explanation.en: Defining relative clauses identify and define the person or thing to which we are referring. Commas are not used with this type of clause.

options.text: It is a defining relative clause.

questions.text: Those are our neighbors whose house just burned down.

explanation.es: Hay dos tipos de claúsulas relativas, las que definen al sustantivo (identifican a que cosa o persona nos referimos) y las que no lo definen ( sólo agregan información adicional). Se pone coma para separar la claúsula relativa del resto de la oración.
explanation.en: Defining relative clauses identify and define the person or thing to which we are referring. Commas are not used with this type of clause.

options.text: It is a defining relative clause.

explanation.en: Non-defining relative clauses add additional information to a sentence and are always found between commas.

explanation.es: "But" es una conjunción coordinante que significa "pero".  Conjunciones coordinantes siempre van entre frases o palabras con el mismo valor.
explanation.en: "But" is a coordinating conjunction that contrasts two words or clauses. Coordinating conjunctions are always found between the words or clauses they connect.

questions.text: She speaks slowly _____ clearly.

explanation.es: "And" es una conjunción coordinante que significa "y".  Las conjunciones coordinantes siempre van entre frases o palabras con el mismo valor.
explanation.en: "And" is a coordinating conjunction that adds information. Coordinating conjunctions are always located between the words or clauses that they connect.

questions.text: She speaks fast, _____ I understand her.

explanation.es: "But" es una conjunción coordinante que significa "pero".  Las conjunciones coordinantes siempre van entre frases o palabras con el mismo valor.
explanation.en: "But" is a coordinating conjunction that contrasts two words or clauses. Coordinating conjunctions are always found between the words or clauses they connect.

questions.text: What would you like, coffee _____ tea?

explanation.es: "Or" es una conjunción coordinante que significa "o".  Las conjunciones coordinantes siempre van entre frases o palabras con el mismo valor.
explanation.en: "Or" is a coordinating conjunction which is used to present an alternative or to introduce another choice or possibility. Coordinating conjunctions are always located between the words or clauses that they connect.

questions.text: I have been living in Barcelona _____ 1999.

explanation.es: "Since" significa "desde" y es una conjunción subordinante que se utiliza cuando una de las frases depende de la otra (frase subordinada). La frase subordinada no tiene sentido sin la otra.
explanation.en: "Since" is a subordinating conjunction that is used with a specific moment in the past. As a subordinating conjunction, it is found before the subordinate (or dependent) clause.

questions.text: _____ it stopped raining, we went to the beach.

explanation.es: "After" es una conjunción subordinante que significa "después" y que se utiliza cuando una de las frases depende de la otra (frase subordinada). La frase subordinada no tiene sentido sin la otra.
explanation.en: "After" is a subordinating conjunction and is therefore found in front of the subordinating clause.

questions.text: He went to the Alps on holiday this year, _____ he usually prefers the beach.

explanation.es: "However" es una conjunción coordinante y siempre van entre frases o palabras con el mismo valor. Significa "sin embargo".
explanation.en: "However" is a coordinating conjunction that has a similar meaning to "but". As a coordinating conjunction, if is found between the words or phrases that it connects.

questions.text: John is not _____ fast _____ you.

explanation.es: "As...as" significa "tan...como" y es una conjunción correlativa que se utiliza para comparaciones.  Los conjunciones correlativas rodean a un adjetivo o a un adverbio.
explanation.en: "As...as" is a correlative conjunction used in making comparisons.

questions.text: Heather is moving to Florida _____ she got a new job.

explanation.es: "Because" significa "porque" y es una conjunción subordinante.  Utilizamos las conjunciones subordinantes cuando una de las frases depende de la otra.
explanation.en: "Because" is a subordinating conjunction which is used to explain or give reason and is found in front of the subordinating clause.

questions.text: I am saving my money _____ I can buy a new car.

explanation.es: "So that" es una conjunción compleja y subordinante y significa "para que". Utilizamos las conjunciones subordinantes cuando una de las frases depende de la otra.
explanation.en: "So that" is a compound conjunction used for giving an explanation or a reason. As a subordinating conjunction, it is found before the subordinating clause.

explanation.es: Los adjetivos que terminan en "-ed" indican emociones y los adjetivos que terminan en "-ing" indican una característica de algo o alguien.
explanation.en: Adjectives ending in "-ed" indicate feelings and adjectives "ending" in "-ing" describe a characteristic of something or someone.

questions.text: Some people think dogs are ____ cats.

explanation.es: El comparativo de "good" es "better".
explanation.en: The comparative of "good" is "better".

questions.text: The game was _____. It was _____ game I've ever seen.

explanation.es: Los adjetivos que terminan en "-ing" indican o describen una característica de algo o alguien.
explanation.en: Adjectives ending in “-ing” indicate or describe a characteristic of something or someone.

questions.text: He works ____ a consultant.

explanation.es: Cuando es una preposición, "as" significa el papel o profesión de algo o alguien.

questions.text: Sheila is  ____ Lisa.

questions.text: Frank drives ____ his brother.

questions.text: than / father / is / mother / is / my / younger / my

explanation.es: En las comparaciones de superioridad, el adjetivo es seguido por “than”.
explanation.en: In comparisons of superiority the adjective is followed by "than".

answer.raw: My mother is younger than my father. | My father is younger than my mother.
accepted: My mother is younger than my father.
My father is younger than my mother.
accepted_placeholders: My mother is younger than my father.
My father is younger than my mother.

questions.text: cheapest / store / table / is / the / in / this / the

explanation.es: En el grado superlativo se usa “the” delante del adjetivo en la forma superlativa.
explanation.en: In the superlative, we use "the" in front of the adjective in the superlative form.

answer.raw: This table is the cheapest in the store. | This is the cheapest table in the store.
accepted: This table is the cheapest in the store.
This is the cheapest table in the store.
accepted_placeholders: This table is the cheapest in the store.
This is the cheapest table in the store.

questions.text: as / cookies / good / mine / as / are / Sue's / not

explanation.es: Para formar comparativos de inferioridad usamos la conjunción “not as…as” y el adjetivo está en el grado positivo.
explanation.en: To form comparatives of inferiority we use the conjunction "not as...as" with the adjective in the positive grade.

answer.raw: Sue's cookies are not as good as mine.
accepted: Sue's cookies are not as good as mine.
accepted_placeholders: Sue's cookies are not as good as mine.

questions.text: football / more / than / basketball / is / popular

explanation.es: Al hacer comparaciones de superioridad, si el adjetivo es de dos sílabas o má,s usamos "more" antes del adjetivo.
explanation.en: When making comparisons of superiority, if the adjective is two syllables or more we use "more" before the adjective.

answer.raw: Football is more popular than basketball. | Basketball is more popular than football.
accepted: Football is more popular than basketball.
Basketball is more popular than football.
accepted_placeholders: Football is more popular than basketball.
Basketball is more popular than football.

questions.text: more / is / dress / a lot / this / than / black / expensive / the / dress

explanation.es: Podemos modificar una comparación utilizando una cuantificador como "a lot".
explanation.en: We can modify a comparison using a quantifier such as "a lot"

answer.raw: This dress is a lot more expensive than the black dress. | The black dress is a lot more expensive than this dress.
accepted: This dress is a lot more expensive than the black dress.
The black dress is a lot more expensive than this dress.
accepted_placeholders: This dress is a lot more expensive than the black dress.
The black dress is a lot more expensive than this dress.

questions.text: sister / faster / Sue / than / her / speaks

explanation.es: En las comparaciones de superioridad, el adjetivo es seguido por “than”.
explanation.en: In comparisons of superiority the adjective is followed by "than".

answer.raw: Sue speaks faster than her sister.
accepted: Sue speaks faster than her sister.
accepted_placeholders: Sue speaks faster than her sister.

questions.text: My mother is ____ than my father.

explanation.es: El grado comparativo está formado por añadir "-er" al final del adjetivo o con el uso de "more". Para comparativos de superioridad el adjetivo es seguido por "than".
explanation.en: The comparative is formed by adding "-er" to the end of the adjective or with the use of "more." In comparisons of superiority, the adjective is followed by “than”.

questions.text: What is ____ movie you have ever seen?

explanation.es: El grado superlativo denota la cualidad en el grado más alto e igual que en español, se usa "the" delante del adjetivo.
explanation.en: The superlative grade denotes a quality at its highest degree and is formed using the article “the” in front of the superlative form of the adjective.

questions.text: That movie was bad, but it wasn't ____ I have ever seen.

explanation.es: El grado superlativo denota la cualidad en el grado más alto e igual que en español, se usa "the" delante del adjetivo. También, el adjetivo "bad" es irregular.
explanation.en: The superlative grade denotes a quality at its highest degree and is formed using the article “the” in front of the superlative form of the adjective. Also, in this case, the adjective "bad" is irregular.

questions.text: Rachel's hair is not as ____ as Sarah's.

explanation.es: Para comparativos de inferioridad, usamos "not as...as" o "less" delante del adjetivo.
explanation.en: Comparisons of inferiority are formed with the conjunctions “not as…as” or “less…than”. In both cases, the adjective is in the positive grade.

questions.text: Yesterday's exam was ____ than the one last month.

explanation.es: Para adjetivos de dos o más sílabas en el grado comparativo añadiremos "more" o "less" en vez de "-er".
explanation.en: For adjectives of two syllables or more in the comparative, we add "more" or "less" before the adjective, instead of the "-er" termination.

questions.text: Peter is as ____ as Alex.

explanation.es: En comparativos de igualdad, el adjetivo va entre "as...as".
explanation.en: We use the conjunction “as…as” to form comparisons of equality.

questions.text: I think Mary is ____ woman I have ever seen.

explanation.es: Para adjetivos de dos o más sílabas en el grado superlativo añadiremos "most" o "least" en vez de "-est".
explanation.en: For adjectives of two syllables or more in the superlative, we use "the most" or "the least" instead of the "-est" termination.

questions.text: Her room is only a little bit ____ than mine.

explanation.es: Para comparativos de superioridad o inferioridad, el adjetivo va seguido por "than" y puede ser modificado con un cuantificador.
explanation.en: In comparisons of superiority or inferiority, the adjective, is followed by “than” and can be modified with a quantifier.

questions.text: He is ____ when he is playing football.

explanation.es: Si el adjetivo es posesivo, no se usa "the". Además no se usa "the" si comparamos algo con si mismo.
explanation.en: If the adjective is possessive, we don’t use “the”. In addition, we don’t use “the” when comparing something with itself.

questions.text: Michael's house is ____ from the train than Betty's.

explanation.es: "Far" es irregular.
explanation.en: "Far" is an irregular adjective.

instruction.es: Escribe la forma de adverbio comparativa o superlativa del adjetivo entre paréntesis.

explanation.es: Para formar la forma comparativa de los adverbios terminados en "ly", utilizamos los cuantificadores "more" para comparativas de superioridad o "less" para inferioridad. Para la forma superlativa utilizamos "most" (superioridad) o "least" (inferioridad).
explanation.en: To form the comparative form of adverbs ending in “-ly”, we use the quantifiers “more” for comparatives of superiority or “less” for comparisons of inferiority.

questions.text: You need to push ____ (hard).

explanation.es: Algunos adverbios mantienen la misma forma que el adjetivo. Para estos adverbios, igual que en sus formas adjetivas, añadimos "-er" para formar el comparativo y "-est" para formar el superlativo.
explanation.en: Some adverbs maintain the same form as the adjective. For these adverbs, as with their adjective forms, we add “-er” to form the comparative and “-est” to form the superlative.

explanation.es: Las formas comparativas y superlativas de los adverbios irregulares son las mismas que las formas del adjetivo.
explanation.en: The comparative and superlative forms of irregular adverbs are the same as their adjective forms.

questions.text: The ____ (fast) car wins.

explanation.es: Algunos adverbios mantienen la misma forma que el adjetivo. Para estos adverbios, igual que en sus formas adjetivas, añadimos "-er" para formar el comparativo y "-est" para formar el superlativo.
explanation.en: Some adverbs maintain the same form as the adjective. For these adverbs, as with their adjective forms, we add “-er” to form the comparative and “-est” to form the superlative.

questions.text: David is very generous, but at times he spends his money ____ (careless) than he should.

explanation.es: Para formar la forma comparativa de los adverbios terminados en "ly", utilizamos los cuantificadores "more" para comparativas de superioridad o "less" para inferioridad.
explanation.en: To form the comparative form of adverbs ending in “-ly”, we use the quantifiers “more” for comparatives of superiority or “less” for comparisons of inferiority.

questions.text: Mario likes to have fun. He does his work ____ (serious) than Chris.

explanation.es: Para formar la forma comparativa de los adverbios terminados en "ly", utilizamos los cuantificadores "more" para comparativas de superioridad o "less" para inferioridad. Para la forma superlativa utilizamos "most" (superioridad) o "least" (inferioridad).
explanation.en: To form the comparative form of adverbs ending in “-ly”, we use the quantifiers “more” for comparatives of superiority or “less” for comparisons of inferiority.

questions.text: Johnny speaks ____ (bad) English than Devon.

explanation.es: Las formas comparativas y superlativas de los adverbios irregulares son las mismas que las formas del adjetivo.
explanation.en: The comparative and superlative forms of irregular adverbs are the same as their adjective forms.

questions.text: Meghan has changed the ____ (radical) since high school. I didn't even recognize her!

explanation.es: Para formar la forma comparativa de los adverbios terminados en "ly", utilizamos los cuantificadores "more" para comparativas de superioridad o "less" para inferioridad. Para la forma superlativa utilizamos "most" (superioridad) o "least" (inferioridad).
explanation.en: To form the comparative form of adverbs ending in “-ly”, we use the quantifiers “more” for comparatives of superiority or “less” for comparisons of inferiority. For the superlative form we use “most” (superiority) or “least” (inferiority).

questions.text: He finished the test ____ (quick) than the rest of the class.

explanation.es: Para formar la forma comparativa de los adverbios terminados en "ly", utilizamos los cuantificadores "more" para comparativas de superioridad o "less" para inferioridad. Para la forma superlativa utilizamos "most" (superioridad) o "least" (inferioridad).
explanation.en: To form the comparative form of adverbs ending in “-ly”, we use the quantifiers “more” for comparatives of superiority or “less” for comparisons of inferiority. For the superlative form we use “most” (superiority) or “least” (inferiority).

instruction.es: Completa la frase con "like" o "as".
instruction.en: Complete the sentences with "like" or "as".

questions.text: He has lots of hobbies, such _____ photography, painting and cycling.

explanation.es: Se usa "such as" para presentar ejemplos.
explanation.en: We use "such as" to present examples.

questions.text: My mother and I have the same nose. I look _____ my mother.

explanation.es: Cuando se utiliza "like" con el verbo "look" significa "parecer" o "parecerse".
explanation.en: We use "like" with the verb "look" to indicate that two people or things resemble or are very similar in appearance to one another.

questions.text: _____ they had predicted, it started raining.

explanation.es: A diferencia de "like", "as" está seguido por un sujeto y un verbo.
explanation.en: As opposed to "like", "as" is followed by a subject and a verb.

questions.text: I am your mother! And _____ your mother I'm asking you to clean your room.

explanation.es: Cuando es una preposición, "as" significa el papel o profesión de algo o alguien.
explanation.en: When it is used as a preposition, "as" means the profession or role of something or someone.

questions.text: It is just _____ John to forget her birthday.

explanation.es: We also use "like" to say that something is typical of someone.
explanation.en: También, se utiliza "like" para decir que algo es típico de alguien.

questions.text: _____ me, my brother doesn't like reading.

explanation.es: "Like" es una preposición que significa "parecido a" o "lo mismo de". Como una preposición, "like" está seguida por un sustantivo o un pronombre.
explanation.en: "Like" is a preposition which means "similar to" or "the same as". As a preposition, "like" is followed by a noun or pronoun.

instruction.es: Escribe los verbos que están entre paréntesis en  gerundio o  infinitivo.
instruction.en: Write the verb in parenthesis in the gerund or infinitive.

questions.text: I hope ____ (go) to England next year.

explanation.es: Cuando un verbo sigue otro verbo, siempre necesitamos usar el infinitivo o el gerundio. Normalmente usamos el infinitivo después de unos verbos y el gerundio después de otros. Siempre usamos el infinitivo después del verbo "to hope".
explanation.en: When one verb is followed by another verb, that verb must be in the gerund or the infinitive. Generally, we use the infinitive after some verbs and the gerund after others. We always use the infinitive after the verb "to hope".

questions.text: They enjoy ____ (work) together.

explanation.es: Siempre usamos el gerundio después del verbo "to enjoy".
explanation.en: We can only use the gerund after the verb "to enjoy".

questions.text: David quit ____ (smoke) a year ago.

explanation.es: Siempre usamos el gerundio después del verbo "to quit".
explanation.en: We always use the gerund after the verb "to quit".

questions.text: Do you want ____ (study) with me tonight?

explanation.es: Siempre usamos el infinitivo después del verbo "to want".
explanation.en: We always use the infinitive after the verb "to want".

questions.text: We were anxious ____ (take) the exam.

explanation.es: Se usa el infinitivo después de adjetivos.
explanation.en: We use the infinitive after adjectives.

questions.text: He always takes a nap after ____ (eat) a big meal.

explanation.es: Después de preposiciones solo podemos usar el gerundio.
explanation.en: After prepositions we can only use the gerund.

questions.text: I have no experience in ____ (work) with children.

explanation.es: Se usa el gerundio después de nombres.
explanation.en: We use the gerund after nouns and prepositions.

questions.text: We're looking forward to ____ (meet) you!

explanation.es: Se usa el gerundio después de expresiones o verbos frasales.
explanation.en: We use the gerund after expressions and phrasal verbs.

questions.text: Carla doesn't feel like ____ (cook) tonight, so we are going to a restaurant.

explanation.es: Se usa el gerundio después de expresiones o verbos frasales.
explanation.en: We use the gerund after expressions and phrasal verbs.

questions.text: Sue was relieved ____ (learn) that she didn't have cancer.

explanation.es: Se usa el infinitivo después de adjetivos.
explanation.en: We use the infinitive after adjectives.

questions.text: We can't afford _____ (go) on holiday.

explanation.es: Cuando un verbo sigue otro verbo, siempre necesitamos usar el infinitivo o el gerundio. Normalmente usamos el infinitivo después de unos verbos y el gerundio después de otros. Siempre usamos el infinitivo después del verbo "to afford".
explanation.en: When one verb is followed by another verb, that verb must be in the gerund or the infinitive. Generally, we use the infinitive after some verbs and the gerund after others. We always use the infinitive after the verb "to afford".

explanation.en: Closed questions are constructed using the auxiliary verb "do" and require a "yes" or "no" response.

accepted: Do you have children? Yes I do.

questions.text: _____ of you two can help me?

explanation.es: Usamos which" cuando las opciones posibles son limitadas a un pequeño número (quizás 2, 3 o 4) y hay que seleccionar una entre ellas.
explanation.en: We use "which" when the possible answers are limited to a small quantity (perhaps 2, 3 or 4) and it is necessary to choose among them.

questions.text: _____ is your favorite food?

explanation.es: Se utiliza "what" para hacer una pregunta cuando hay un número desconocido o infinitas posibilidades de respuestas u opciones.
explanation.en: "What" is used to ask a question when there is an unknown or infinite number of possible answers or options. It is used for asking questions in general.
