explanation.en: “What” is used to ask a question when there is an unknown or infinite number of possible answers or options. It is used for asking questions in general.

questions.text: ____ type of tea do you like?

explanation.es: Se utiliza "what" para hacer una pregunta cuando hay un número desconocido o infinitas posibilidades de respuestas.
explanation.en: “What” is used to ask a question when there is an unknown or infinite number of possible answers or options. It is used for asking questions in general.

questions.text: We have cola or juice. ____ do you prefer?

explanation.es: En contraste con "what", usamos "which" cuando las respuestas posibles son limitadas a un pequeño número (quizás 2, 3 o 4) y hay que seleccionar entre ellas.
explanation.en: In contrast to “what”, we use “which” when the possible answers are limited to a small quantity (perhaps 2, 3 or 4) and it is necessary to choose among them. This interrogative pronoun can be used with both things and people.

questions.text: I can't decide ____ to wear.

explanation.es: Se utiliza "what" para hacer una pregunta cuando hay un número desconocido o infinitas posibilidades de respuestas.
explanation.en: “What” is used to ask a question when there is an unknown or infinite number of possible answers or options. It is used for asking questions in general.

questions.text: I can't decide ____ dress to wear, the blue one or the red one.

explanation.es: En contraste con "what", usamos "which" cuando las respuestas posibles son limitadas a un pequeño número (quizás 2, 3 o 4) y hay que seleccionar entre ellas.
explanation.en: In contrast to “what”, we use “which” when the possible answers are limited to a small quantity (perhaps 2, 3 or 4) and it is necessary to choose among them. This interrogative pronoun can be used with both things and people.

instruction.es: Completa las frases con la forma correcta del verbo que se encuentra entre paréntesis.
instruction.en: Complete the sentences with the correct form of the verb in parenthesis.

questions.text: Henry ____ (live) in New York for 3 years.

explanation.es: Usamos el pasado simple para narraciones o acciones de períodos de largo tiempo en el pasado, como el pretérito imperfecto español.
explanation.en: The past simple is also used for narrations or actions that occurred over a long period of time in the past.

questions.text: Yesterday was a long day.  I _____ (work) all afternoon, _____ (go) to class in the evening and then _____ (have) dinner with my parents.

explanation.es: Se usa el pasado simple para una serie de acciones en el pasado.
explanation.en: The past simple is used to talk about a series of actions that occurred in the past.

questions.text: She _____ (make) dinner when the boys _____ (come) home.

explanation.es: El pasado continuo lo utilizamos para una acción larga que en el pasado fue interrumpida (pasado simple).  "When" señala el uso del pasado simple.
explanation.en: The past continuous is used for an action or event that occurred over a long period of time in the past and that was interrupted (past simple).

questions.text: In 1996, Bob ____ (work) in a hospital.

explanation.es: Se usa el pasado continuo para hablar sobre acciones en un tiempo específico en el pasado.
explanation.en: We use the past continuous to talk about actions or events that happened at a specific time in the past.

questions.text: When I was young, we ____ (not/have) computers.

explanation.es: Se utiliza el pasado simple para hablar de generalidades o hechos del pasado.
explanation.en: We use the past simple to speak about facts or things in the past in general.

questions.text: What _____ you _____ (do) last night when I _____ (call)?

explanation.es: El pasado continuo lo utilizamos para una acción larga que en el pasado fue interrumpido (pasado simple). "When" señala el uso del pasado simple.
explanation.en: The past continuous is used for an action or event that occurred over a long period of time in the past and that was interrupted (past simple).

questions.text: It _____ (start) to rain while he _____ (walk) the dog.

explanation.es: El pasado continuo lo utilizamos para una acción larga que en el pasado fue interrumpida (pasado simple). Después de "while" usamos el pasado continuo.
explanation.en: The past continuous is used for an action or event that occurred over a period of time in the past and that was interrupted (past simple).

questions.text: My husband _____ (watch) the football game while I _____ (read) a book.

explanation.es: Se usa el pasado continuo para dos acciones que estaban ocurriendo al mismo tiempo en el pasado.
explanation.en: The past continuous is used for two actions that were occurring simultaneously in the past.

answer.raw: watched, was reading | was watching, was reading

questions.text: Steve ____ (buy) a new car last year.

explanation.es: El pasado simple se utiliza para hablar de una acción concreta que comenzó y acabó en el pasado. En este caso equivale al pretérito indefinido español. Generalmente, lo usamos con adverbios de tiempo como "last year", "yesterday", "last night"...
explanation.en: The past simple is used to talk about a specific action or event that began and ended in the past. It is generally used with adverbs of time such as “last year”, “yesterday”, “last night”…

questions.text: I ____ (play) volleyball with my friends at 3pm yesterday.

explanation.es: Se usa el pasado continuo para hablar sobre acciones en un tiempo específico en el pasado.
explanation.en: We use the past continuous to talk about actions or events that happened at a specific time in the past.

questions.text: I ____ (meet) her in 2005.

explanation.es: El pasado simple se utiliza para hablar de una acción concreta que comenzó y acabó en el pasado. En este caso equivale al pretérito indefinido español.
explanation.en: The past simple is used to talk about a specific action or event that began and ended in the past.

instruction.es: Escribe las palabras en el orden correcto.
instruction.en: Write the words in the correct order.

questions.text: did / bag / where / buy / you / that / ?

explanation.es: En frases interrogativas, el verbo auxiliar va antes del verbo principal que se quede en el infinitivo.
explanation.en: In interrogative sentences, the auxiliary verb is found before the principal verb which is in the infinitive.

answer.raw: Where did you buy that bag?
accepted: Where did you buy that bag?
accepted_placeholders: Where did you buy that bag?

questions.text: swam / beach / I / the / at / sea / in / the

explanation.es: Muchos verbos son irregulares en el pasado.
explanation.en: Many verbs are irregular in the past tense.

answer.raw: I swam in the sea at the beach.
accepted: I swam in the sea at the beach.
accepted_placeholders: I swam in the sea at the beach.

questions.text: football / children / the / park / the / in / played

explanation.es: La forma de los verbos en el pasado es igual para todos los pronombres.
explanation.en: The past tense form of the verb is the same for all pronouns.

answer.raw: The children played football in the park.
accepted: The children played football in the park.
accepted_placeholders: The children played football in the park.

questions.text: ? / keys / you / did / find / your

explanation.es: En frases interrogativas, el verbo auxiliar va antes del verbo principal que se quede en el infinitivo.
explanation.en: In interrogative sentences, the auxiliary verb is found before the principal verb which is in the infinitive.

questions.text: the / left / we / hotel / bags / our / at

explanation.es: Muchos verbos son irregulares en el pasado.
explanation.en: Many verbs are irregular in the past tense.

answer.raw: We left our bags at the hotel.
accepted: We left our bags at the hotel.
accepted_placeholders: We left our bags at the hotel.

questions.text: school / she / to / didn't / yesterday / go

explanation.es: El pasado simple se utiliza para hablar de una acción concreta que comenzó y acabó en el pasado.
explanation.en: The past simple is used for speaking about an action that began and ended in the past.

answer.raw: She didn't go to school yesterday.
accepted: She didn't go to school yesterday.
accepted_placeholders: She didn't go to school yesterday.

questions.text: at / meeting / he / was / the / ?

explanation.es: El verbo auxiliar "did" no es necesario cuando el verbo principal es "to be".
explanation.en: The auxiliary verb "did" is not necessary when the principal verb is "to be".

questions.text: night / we / good / went / a / to / last / restaurant

explanation.es: El pasado simple se utiliza para hablar de una acción concreta que comenzó y acabó en el pasado.
explanation.en: The past simple is used for speaking about an action that began and ended in the past.

answer.raw: We went to a good restaurant last night.
accepted: We went to a good restaurant last night.
accepted_placeholders: We went to a good restaurant last night.

questions.text: study / he / exam / didn't / the / for

explanation.es: En las frases negativas el verbo auxiliar va en pasado y el verbo principal se queda en el infinitivo.
explanation.en: In negative sentences, the auxiliary verb is in the past and the principal verb stays in the infinitive.

answer.raw: He didn't study for the exam.
accepted: He didn't study for the exam.
accepted_placeholders: He didn't study for the exam.

instruction.es: Completa las frases con la forma correcta del verbo que se encuentra entre paréntesis.
instruction.en: Complete the sentences with the correct form of the verb in parenthesis.

questions.text: Last night we ____ (walk) to the cinema.

explanation.es: Para formar el pasado simple con verbos regulares, usamos el infinitivo y añadimos la terminación "-ed". La forma es la misma para todas las formas personales (I, you, he, she, it, we, they).
explanation.en: To form the past simple of regular verbs we add the ending “-ed” to the verb. The form is the same for all persons (I, you, he, she, it, we, they).

questions.text: Sam ____ (stop) the car to take a picture.

explanation.es: Si el verbo termina en una vocal corta y una consonante (excepto "y" o "w"), doblamos la consonante final.
explanation.en: If the verb ends in a short vowel plus a consonant (except “y” o “w”), we double the final consonant.

questions.text: I ____ (study) for the exam for three hours.

explanation.es: En verbos terminados en consonante seguida de "y", se cambia la "y" por "i".
explanation.en: When the verb ends in a consonant and “y”, the “y” becomes an “i”.

questions.text: They ____ (be) happy to be home.

explanation.es: "To be" es irregular en el pasado.
explanation.en: "To be" is irregular in the past.

questions.text: Sally _____ (be) disappointed she _____ (miss) the party.

explanation.es: "To be" es irregular en el pasado y cambia dependiendo de la forma personal (I, he, she, it: was; you, we, they: were).
explanation.en: "To be" is irregular in the past.

questions.text: When I was young, we always ____ (go) to Florida for the summer.

explanation.es: "To go" es irregular en el pasado.
explanation.en: "To go" is irregular in the past.

questions.text: Dan ____ (not/work) last week.

explanation.es: En frases negativas en pasado, el verbo auxiliar "to do" cambia a pasado ("did" + "not")  y el verbo principal se queda en infinitivo.
explanation.en: In negative sentences in the past simple the auxiliary verb is conjugated (“did” + "not") and the principal verb remains in the infinitive.

questions.text: _____ you _____ (wash) the dishes?

explanation.es: En frases interrogativas en pasado, el verbo auxiliar "to do" cambia a pasado ("did") y el verbo principal se queda en infinitivo.
explanation.en: In interrogative sentences, the auxiliary verb is conjugated (“did”) and the principal verb remains in the infinitive.

questions.text: I ____ (dream) I could fly last night.

explanation.es: "To dream" es irregular en el pasado.
explanation.en: "To dream" is irregular in the past.

questions.text: We ____ (meet) them at the restaurant.

explanation.es: "To meet" es irregular en el pasado.
explanation.en: "To meet" is irregular in the past.

questions.text: _____ you _____ (find) your book?

explanation.es: En frases interrogativas en pasado, el verbo auxiliar "to do" cambia a pasado ("did") y el verbo principal se queda en infinitivo.
explanation.en: In interrogative sentences, the auxiliary verb is conjugated (“did”) and the principal verb remains in the infinitive.

instruction.es: Escribe las palabras en el orden correcto.
instruction.en: Write the words in the correct order.

questions.text: cooking / she / arrived / was / I / when

explanation.es: El pasado continuo se usa para una acción en desarrollo en el pasado cuando otra acción la interrumpe. La acción que interrumpe está en el pasado simple.
explanation.en: The past continuous is used for an action in progress in the past and that was interrupted. The interrupting action is in the past simple.

answer.raw: She was cooking when I arrived.
accepted: She was cooking when I arrived.
accepted_placeholders: She was cooking when I arrived.

questions.text: doing / what / 3 o'clock / you / were / at / ?

explanation.es: Se usa el pasado continuo para hablar sobre acciones en un tiempo específico en el pasado.
explanation.en: We use the past continuous to speak about actions in a specific moment in the past.

answer.raw: What were you doing at 3 o'clock?
accepted: What were you doing at 3 o'clock?
accepted_placeholders: What were you doing at 3 o'clock?

questions.text: students / teacher / listening / the / weren't / the / to

explanation.es: Para formar el pasado continuo se utiliza el verbo auxiliar “to be” en el pasado y el verbo+ing.
explanation.en: To form the past continuous we use the auxiliary verb "to be" in the past and the verb+ing.

answer.raw: The students weren't listening to the teacher.
accepted: The students weren't listening to the teacher.
accepted_placeholders: The students weren't listening to the teacher.

questions.text: film / watching / we / called / when / you / were / a

explanation.es: El pasado continuo se usa para una acción en desarrollo en el pasado cuando otra acción la interrumpe. La acción que interrumpe está en el pasado simple.
explanation.en: The past continuous is used for an action in progress in the past and that was interrupted. The interrupting action is in the past simple.

answer.raw: We were watching a film when you called.
accepted: We were watching a film when you called.
accepted_placeholders: We were watching a film when you called.

questions.text: ? / bus / long / you / how / were / the / for / waiting

explanation.es: Como las frases interrogativas en otros tiempos, el orden de las palabras cambia.
explanation.en: As with interrogative sentences in other tenses, the word order changes.

answer.raw: How long were you waiting for the bus?
accepted: How long were you waiting for the bus?
accepted_placeholders: How long were you waiting for the bus?

questions.text: home / raining / so / it / we / was / went

explanation.es: Para formar el pasado continuo se utiliza el verbo auxiliar “to be” en el pasado y el verbo+ing.
explanation.en: To form the past continuous we use the auxiliary verb "to be" in the past and the verb+ing.

answer.raw: It was raining so we went home.
accepted: It was raining so we went home.
accepted_placeholders: It was raining so we went home.

questions.text: arrived / doing / homework / he / was / you / when/ ?

explanation.es: El pasado continuo se usa para una acción en desarrollo en el pasado cuando otra acción la interrumpe. La acción que interrumpe está en el pasado simple.
explanation.en: The past continuous is used for an action in progress in the past and that was interrupted. The interrupting action is in the past simple.

answer.raw: Was he doing homework when you arrived?
accepted: Was he doing homework when you arrived?
accepted_placeholders: Was he doing homework when you arrived?

questions.text: noise / while / reading / was / newspaper / the / l / heard / a / I

explanation.es: El pasado continuo se usa para una acción en desarrollo en el pasado cuando otra acción la interrumpe. La acción que interrumpe está en el pasado simple.
explanation.en: The past continuous is used for an action in progress in the past and that was interrupted. The interrupting action is in the past simple.

answer.raw: While I was reading the newspaper I heard a noise. | I heard a noise while I was reading the newspaper.
accepted: While I was reading the newspaper I heard a noise.
I heard a noise while I was reading the newspaper.
accepted_placeholders: While I was reading the newspaper I heard a noise.
I heard a noise while I was reading the newspaper.

questions.text: called / I / sleeping / wasn't / when / you

explanation.en: The past continuous is used for an action in progress in the past and that was interrupted. The interrupting action is in the past simple.

answer.raw: I wasn't sleeping when you called.
accepted: I wasn't sleeping when you called.

instruction.es: Completa las frases con la forma correcta del verbo que se encuentra entre paréntesis.

questions.text: We ____ (play) video games yesterday at 3 o'clock.

explanation.es: El pasado continuo se utiliza para acciones que ocurrieron en un momento específico en el pasado. Como el presente continuo, se forma con el verbo auxiliar “to be” y el verbo+ing.
explanation.en: The past continuous tense is used for actions that occurred at a specific time in the past. Like the present continuous, it is formed using the verb “to be” as well as the verb+ing.

questions.text: They ____ (not/do) their homework yesterday at 3 o'clock

explanation.es: El pasado continuo se utiliza para acciones que ocurrieron en un momento específico en el pasado. Como el presente continuo, se forma con el verbo auxiliar “to be” y el verbo+ing.
explanation.en: The past continuous tense is used for actions that occurred at a specific time in the past. Like the present continuous, it is formed using the verb “to be” as well as the verb+ing.

questions.text: What _____ he _____ (watch) on television when you arrived?

explanation.es: El pasado continuo se utiliza para acciones que ocurrieron en un momento específico en el pasado. Como el presente continuo, se forma con el verbo auxiliar “to be” y el verbo+ing.
explanation.en: The past continuous tense is used for actions that occurred at a specific time in the past. Like the present continuous, it is formed using the verb “to be” as well as the verb+ing.

questions.text: Howard ____ (like) the movie.

explanation.es: Hay unos verbos que no solemos usar en los tiempos continuos como el verbo emotivo "like".
explanation.en: there are some verbs which may not be used in the continuous tenses, such as the emotive verb "like".

questions.text: My parents ____ (work) when I came home.

explanation.es: El pasado continuo se utiliza para acciones que ocurrieron en un momento específico en el pasado. Como el presente continuo, se forma con el verbo auxiliar “to be” y el verbo+ing.
explanation.en: The past continuous tense is used for actions that occurred at a specific time in the past. Like the present continuous, it is formed using the verb “to be” as well as the verb+ing.

explanation.es: El pasado continuo se utiliza para acciones que ocurrieron en un momento específico en el pasado. Como el presente continuo, se forma con el verbo auxiliar “to be” y el verbo+ing.
explanation.en: The past continuous tense is used for actions that occurred at a specific time in the past. Like the present continuous, it is formed using the verb “to be” as well as the verb+ing.

questions.text: She ____ (listen) to music when I called.

explanation.en: The past continuous tense is used for actions that occurred at a specific time in the past. Like the present continuous, it is formed using the verb “to be” as well as the verb+ing.

questions.text: _____ it _____ (rain) when you left?

instruction.es: Elige las palabras para completar la frase: used to / am used to / usually / didn't use to / did you use to / use to / studying / study
instruction.en: Choose the words to complete the sentence: used to / am used to / usually / didn't use to / did you use to / use to / studying / study

questions.text: We _____ get up late on Sundays. We like to stay in bed and relax.

explanation.es: No utilizamos "used to" para acciones habituales en el presente. En vez de este verbo modal, se usa un adverbio como "usually" o "normally" por ejemplo.
explanation.en: We do not use "used to" for habits or things that happen on a regular basis in the present. Instead of this modal verb, we use adverbs such as "usually" or "normally" in the present.

questions.text: I _____ getting up early.

explanation.es: Cuando se utiliza "used to" como adjetivo significa "estar acostumbrado a".
explanation.en: When we use "used to" as an adjective it means "accustomed to" or "habituated to".

questions.text: _____ have a dog when you were a kid?

explanation.es: Se utiliza "used to" para algo que antes era verdad pero ya no lo es. Como con los otros verbos modales, "used to" está seguido por la forma base del verbo.
explanation.en: "Used to" is used for things that were true in the past, but are no longer true. As with the other modal verbs, "used to" is followed by the base form of the principal verb.

questions.text: I'm not used to _____ late at night.

explanation.es: Cuando "to be used to" está seguido por un verbo, el verbo tiene que estar en gerundio ("‑ing").
explanation.en: When "to be used to" is followed by a verb, the verb must be in the gerund ("-ing").

questions.text: Now he cleans the house every weekend. He _____ help out.

explanation.es: Se utiliza "used to" para algo que antes era verdad pero ya no lo es. Como con los otros verbos modales, "used to" está seguido por la forma base del verbo.
explanation.en: "Used to" is used for things that were true in the past, but are no longer true. As with the other modal verbs, "used to" is followed by the base form of the principal verb.

questions.text: I used to _____ on my bed, but nowadays I concentrate better at a desk.

explanation.es: Se utiliza "used to" para algo que antes era verdad pero ya no lo es. Como con los otros verbos modales, "used to" está seguido por la forma base del verbo.
explanation.en: "Used to" is used for things that were true in the past, but are no longer true. As with the other modal verbs, "used to" is followed by the base form of the principal verb.

instruction.es: Elige la respuesta correcta.
instruction.en: Choose the correct answer.

questions.text: I ____ smoke when I was in my 20s.

explanation.es: El verbo modal "used to" se emplea para indicar algo que occuría o pasaría en el pasado de manera habitual pero ahora no ocurre o pasaba.
explanation.en: The modal verb “used to” is utilized to indicate something that happened on a regular basis in the past. It is also used for things that were true in the past, but are no longer true.

questions.text: I ____ smoke when I drink.

explanation.es: No utilizamos "used to" para acciones habituales en el presente. En vez de este verbo modal, se usa un adverbio como "usually" o"normally" por ejemplo.
explanation.en: We do not use “used to” for habits or things that happen on a regular basis in the present. Instead of this modal verb, we use adverbs such as “usually” or “normally” in the present.

questions.text: I ____ the smell of smoke because my husband smokes.

explanation.es: Cuando se utiliza "used to" como adjetivo con el verbo "to be" significa "estar acostumbrado a". Se usa para cosas que son familiares, normales o comunes y se puede utilizar en cualquier tiempo verbal.
explanation.en: When we use “used to” as an adjective with the verb "to be" it means “accustomed to” or “habituated to”. We use this adjective for things that are familiar, normal or common and it can be used with any verb tense.

questions.text: There was a time when she ____ always work late.

explanation.es: También se puede utilizar el verbo modal "would" para eventos o acciones repetidas en el pasado.
explanation.en: The modal verb “would” is also used for repeated actions or events in the past, but keep in mind that “would” can only be used with actions, not with states of being or habits.

questions.text: He ____ be a vegetarian when he was a kid.

explanation.es: Sólo se puede utilizar "would" en vez de "used to" con acciones, no con estados o hábitos.
explanation.en: “Would” can only be used with actions, not with states of being or habits. Also, “would” cannot be used in the negative.

questions.text: They ____ the silence when they built a restaurant next door.

explanation.es: Cuando utilizamos el verbo "get" indica el proceso de familiarizarse con algo.
explanation.en: When we use the verb “get” instead of “be”, it indicates the process of familiarization.

questions.text: Sally ____ drink a lot of wine.

explanation.es: No se puede utilizar "would" en el negativo y la estructura de frases negativas es: Sujeto + "didn't" + "use to" + verbo...
explanation.en: “Would” cannot be used in the negative. The structure of negative sentences with "used to" is: Subject + "didn't" "use to" + verb...

questions.text: It's a noisy apartment, but I ____ it.

explanation.es: Cuando se utiliza "used to" como adjetivo significa "estar acostumbrado a". Se usa para cosas que son familiares, normales o comunes y se puede utilizar en cualquier tiempo verbal.
explanation.en: When we use “used to” as an adjective it means “accustomed to” or “habituated to”. We use this adjective for things that are familiar, normal or common and it can be used with any verb tense.

questions.text: Bill is used to ____ long days.

explanation.es: Cuando "to be used to" va seguido por un verbo, el verbo tiene que estar en su forma de gerundio ("-ing").
explanation.en: When “to be used to” is followed by a verb, the verb must be in the gerund (“-ing”).

questions.text: By the time John moves in, I ____ living alone.

explanation.es: Cuando se utiliza "used to" como adjetivo significa "estar acostumbrado a". Se usa para cosas que son familiares, normales o comunes y se puede utilizar en cualquier tiempo verbal. En este ejemplo se refiere al futuro.
explanation.en: When we use “used to” as an adjective it means “accustomed to” or “habituated to”. When “to be used to” is followed by a verb, the verb must be in the gerund ("-ing"). This example refers to the future.

instruction.es: Escribe la forma correcta del verbo que está entre paréntesis.
instruction.en: Write the correct form of the verb in parenthesis.

questions.text: I think they ____ (win) the game.

explanation.es: Se usa "will" para predicciones.
explanation.en: We use "will" for predictions.

questions.text: We ____ (buy) a new house next year. [We have started looking]

explanation.es: Se usa "to be going to+ verbo" para planes. Indica la intención de hacer algo.
explanation.en: “To be going to+verb” is used for plans; it indicates the intention to do something.

questions.text: Don't worry, I ____ (call) you tomorrow morning.

explanation.es: Se utiliza "will" para expresar una promesa.
explanation.en: "Will" is used to express a promise.

explanation.en: Henry: "Oh, it's very small. I don't think this will be big enough for my wife and I and our 2 children. Have you got any bigger rooms for four?"

questions.text: The receptionist offers him two adjoining rooms.

explanation.es: Receptionist:  "No, but you could stay in two rooms that have an adjoining door."
explanation.en: Receptionist:  "No, but you could stay in two rooms that have an adjoining door."

questions.text: Henry thinks the two rooms are too expensive.

explanation.es: Receptionist: "The two rooms for two nights will be $320. That is only $50 more than the quadruple one."  Henry: "Yes, that sounds fine."
explanation.en: Receptionist: "The two rooms for two nights will be $320. That is only $50 more than the quadruple one."  Henry: "Yes, that sounds fine."

questions.text: Breakfast is served from 8 to 10:00 am.

explanation.es: Henry: "Thank you. What time is breakfast served?";  Receptionist: "From 7 o'clock to 10 o'clock in our dining room at the end of this hall."
explanation.en: Henry: "Thank you. What time is breakfast served?";  Receptionist: "From 7 o'clock to 10 o'clock in our dining room at the end of this hall."

questions.text: The hotel doesn't offer a buffet breakfast.

explanation.es: Receptionist: "It's a buffet breakfast, there are many hot and cold dishes served."
explanation.en: Receptionist: "It's a buffet breakfast, there are many hot and cold dishes served."

display.es: Test de unidad: El presente perfecto

instruction.es: Ordena las frases.
instruction.en: Order the sentence.

questions.text: finished / I / my / already / have / homework

explanation.es: En el presente perfecto, "already" va entre el verbo auxiliar “to have” y el participio pasado.
explanation.en: In the present perfect, "already" goes between the auxiliary verb "to have" and the past participle.

answer.raw: I have already finished my homework.
accepted: I have already finished my homework.
accepted_placeholders: I have already finished my homework.

questions.text: has / mother / since / working / here / been / 1995 / my

explanation.es: "Since" normalmente va al final de la frase seguido por una expresión del tiempo (hora, día, mes o año).
explanation.en: "Since" normally goes at the end of the sentence followed by an expression of time (time, day, month or year).

answer.raw: My mother has been working here since 1995.
accepted: My mother has been working here since 1995.
accepted_placeholders: My mother has been working here since 1995.

questions.text: last / go / did / where / night / you / ?

explanation.es: El orden de una pregunta con pronombre interrogativo es: pronombre interrogativo+ verbo auxiliar (to do) +sujeto+verbo principal+ sustantivo/adjetivo
explanation.en: The order of a question with an interrogative pronoun is: interrogative pronoun+ auxiliary verb (to do) + subject+ main verb+ noun/adjective

answer.raw: Where did you go last night?
accepted: Where did you go last night?
accepted_placeholders: Where did you go last night?

questions.text: bus / just / missed / the / we / have

explanation.es: En el presente perfecto, "just" va entre el verbo auxiliar “to have” y el participio pasado.
explanation.en: In the present perfect, "just" goes between the auxiliary verb "to have" and the past participle.

answer.raw: We have just missed the bus.
accepted: We have just missed the bus.
accepted_placeholders: We have just missed the bus.

questions.text: eaten / they / haven't / yet

explanation.es: "Yet" siempre va al final de la oración.
explanation.en: "Yet" always goes at the end of the sentence.

questions.text: she / for / ago / two / left / Ireland / days

explanation.es: "Ago" se refiere a un tiempo en el pasado o un tiempo antes del actual y va al final de la frase. Se traduce como "hace" en español y se usa con el pasado simple.
explanation.en: “Ago” means a period of time in the past or before the present and is found at the end of the sentence. It is only used with the past simple.

answer.raw: She left for Ireland two days ago.
accepted: She left for Ireland two days ago.
accepted_placeholders: She left for Ireland two days ago.

questions.text: been / long / waiting / you / have / how / ?

explanation.es: Usamos el presente perfecto continuo con "how long" ("cuánto tiempo").
explanation.en: We use the present perfect continuous with "how long".

answer.raw: How long have you been waiting?
accepted: How long have you been waiting?
accepted_placeholders: How long have you been waiting?

questions.text: gone / ever / have / you / Japan / to / ?

explanation.es: "Ever" se usa normalmente con preguntas en el presente perfecto.
explanation.en: "Ever" is usually used with the present perfect in questions.

answer.raw: Have you ever gone to Japan?
accepted: Have you ever gone to Japan?
accepted_placeholders: Have you ever gone to Japan?

questions.text: have / to / sooner / he / should / gone / doctor / the /

explanation.es: “Should have” + el participio pasado se utiliza cuando algo no ocurrió, pero habría sido mejor si hubiera ocurrido.
explanation.en: “Should have” + past participle are used when something didn’t happen but it would have been better if it had.

answer.raw: He should have gone to the doctor sooner.
accepted: He should have gone to the doctor sooner.
accepted_placeholders: He should have gone to the doctor sooner.

questions.text: studied / they / for / yesterday / hours / three

explanation.es: "Yesterday" normalmente va al final  o al principio de la frase.
explanation.en: "Yesterday" normally goes at the end or at the beginning of a sentence.

answer.raw: They studied for three hours yesterday.
accepted: They studied for three hours yesterday.
accepted_placeholders: They studied for three hours yesterday.

questions.text: day / studying / He's / all / been

explanation.es: Usamos el presente perfecto continuo con "all day" ("todo el día").
explanation.en: We use the present perfect continuous with "all day".

questions.text: long / you / have / how / living / been / here / ?

explanation.es: Usamos este tiempo para acciones que han comenzado en el pasado y que duran todavía en el presente o que acaban de terminar.
explanation.en: We use this tense for actions which began in the past and which continue into the present or which have just finished.

display.es: El presente perfecto y el pasado simple
El presente perfecto y el pasado simple 2

instruction.es: Completa las frases con la forma correcta del verbo que se encuentra entre paréntesis.
instruction.en: Complete the sentences with the correct form of the verb in parenthesis.

questions.text: _____ you _____ (enjoy) the game?

explanation.es: Hay algunos verbos que no podemos usar en los tiempos continuos
explanation.en: There are some verbs which we cannot use in the continuous tenses.

questions.text: How long _____ you _____ (work) here?

explanation.es: Se utiliza el presente perfecto continuo para acciones que empezaron en el pasado pero continúan en el presente.
explanation.en: The present perfect is used for actions or events that are related to the present.

questions.text: The children _____ (grow) a lot since the last time I _____ (see) them.

explanation.es: Se utiliza el presente perfecto para cambios en el tiempo. Se utiliza el pasado simple para acciones que terminaron en el pasado, incluso si han ocurrido en un pasado reciente.
explanation.en: We use the present perfect to express change over time. The past simple is used for actions or events that ended in the past, including the recent past.

questions.text: We _____ never _____ (see) snow.

explanation.es: Usamos el presente perfecto para acciones que todavía no han pasado. El uso del presente perfecto en estos casos indica que aún estamos esperando la acción.
explanation.en: We use the present perfect for actions or events that have not yet happened. The use of the present perfect in these cases indicates that we are still expecting the event or action to occur.

questions.text: What did you have for breakfast this morning? I ____ (have) a coffee and a croissant.

explanation.es: Se utiliza el pasado simple para acciones que terminaron en el pasado, incluso si han ocurrido en un pasado reciente. En este ejemplo, como la pregunta está en el pasado simple, podemos suponer que la mañana ha terminado.
explanation.en: The past simple is used for actions or events that ended in the past, including the recent past. In this example, as the question is in the past simple, we can assume that the morning has ended.

questions.text: _____ you _____ (sing) professionally for long?

explanation.es: Se utiliza el presente perfecto continuo para acciones que empezaron en el pasado pero siguen en el presente.
explanation.en: The present perfect continuous is used for actions or events that began in the past and continue into the present.

questions.text: _____ you _____ (eat) ever eaten snails?

explanation.es: Usamos el presente perfecto para acciones que todavía no han pasado. El uso del presente perfecto en estos casos indica que aún estamos esperando la acción.
explanation.en: We use the present perfect for actions or events that have not yet happened. The use of the present perfect in these cases indicates that we are still expecting the event or action to occur.

questions.text: Last year she ____ (work) a lot.

explanation.es: Se utiliza el pasado simple para acciones que terminaron en el pasado, incluso si han pasado en un pasado reciente.
explanation.en: The past simple is used for actions or events that ended in the past, including the recent past.

instruction.es: Completa las frases con la forma correcta del verbo que se encuentra entre paréntesis.
instruction.en: Complete the sentences with the correct form of the verb in parenthesis.

questions.text: We ____ (go) to the movies last night.

explanation.es: Se utiliza el pasado simple para acciones que terminaron en el pasado, incluso si han pasado en un pasado reciente.
explanation.en: The past simple is used for actions or events that ended in the past, including the recent past.

questions.text: Tom ____ (see) that movie three times already.

explanation.es: Se utiliza el presente perfecto cuando la acción está relacionada con el presente.
explanation.en: The present perfect is used for actions or events that are related to the present.

questions.text: I _____ (read) yesterday's newspaper, but I _____ (read, not) today's.

explanation.es: Se utiliza el pasado simple para acciones que terminaron en el pasado, incluso si han ocurrido en un pasado reciente. Con el presente perfecto la acción está relacionada con el presente.
explanation.en: The past simple is used for actions or events that ended in the past, including the recent past. The present perfect is used for actions or events that are related to the present.

answer.raw: read, have not read | read, haven't read

questions.text: The students ____ (have) eight exams last month.

explanation.es: Se utiliza el pasado simple para acciones que terminaron en el pasado, incluso si han ocurrido en un pasado reciente.
explanation.en: The past simple is used for actions or events that ended in the past, including the recent past.

questions.text: The students ____ (have) eight exams this month. [The month hasn't ended yet.]

explanation.es: Se utiliza el presente perfecto cuando la acción está relacionada con el presente.
explanation.en: The present perfect is used for actions or events that are related to the present.

questions.text: The students ____ (have) eight exams in the last month.

explanation.es: Se utiliza el presente perfecto cuando la acción está relacionada con el presente.  En este ejemplo, la preposición "in" cambia el significado de "last year" a un período de tiempo dentro de la acción ocurrida, no es un tiempo específico.
explanation.en: The present perfect is used for actions or events that are related to the present. With the use of “in”, we are identifying a period of time in which the action or event occurred, but not a specific time.

questions.text: They ____ (practice) for the concert for over three hours now.

explanation.es: Se utiliza el presente perfecto continuo para acciones que empezaron en el pasado pero continúan en el presente. Nos referimos a algo que hemos estado haciendo en un período de tiempo, por lo tanto, usamos las preposiciones de tiempo "for" y "since".
explanation.en: The present perfect continuous is used for actions or events that began in the past and continue into the present.

questions.text: _____ you ever _____ (be) to a professional football game?

explanation.es: Se usa el presente perfecto para describir una experiencia. No lo usamos para acciones específicas.
explanation.en: The present perfect is used to describe an experience. It is not used for specific actions.

questions.text: must / marathon / hard / a / been / have / running

explanation.es: "Must have" se usa para expresar una conclusión lógica sobre algo que ocurrió en el pasado.
explanation.en: "Must have" is used to express a logical conclusion about something that occurred in the past.

answer.raw: Running a marathon must have been hard.
accepted: Running a marathon must have been hard.
accepted_placeholders: Running a marathon must have been hard.

questions.text: didn’t / better / might / we / it / say / have / anything / if / been

explanation.es: "Might have" se utiliza para expresar posibilidad en el pasado. El uso de esto modal también expresa incertidumbre.
explanation.en: "Might have" is used to express possibility in the past. The use of this modal perfect also express uncertainty.

answer.raw: It might have been better if we didn’t say anything.
accepted: It might have been better if we didn’t say anything.
accepted_placeholders: It might have been better if we didn’t say anything.

questions.text: her / studied / more / should / exam / she / have / for

accepted: She should have studied more for her exam.

explanation.en: “Must have” is used to express a logical conclusion about something that occurred in the past. We are unsure of what exactly happened, but based on the evidence, we can come to some conclusion.

instruction.es: Completa las frases con la forma correcta del verbo que se encuentra entre paréntesis.

questions.text: Betty _____ (work) in a bank before she _____ (go) back to school.

explanation.es: Usamos el pasado perfecto para referirnos a una acción o evento que comenzó en el pasado y que es anterior a otra acción también en el pasado (el pasado simple).
explanation.en: We use the past perfect to refer to an event that began in the past and before another action or event in the past. The action or event that that happened first is in the past perfect and the one that follows in the past simple.

questions.text: I _____ already _____ (drink) two cups of coffee by the time my husband _____ (wake up).

explanation.es: Usamos el pasado perfecto para referirnos a una acción o evento que comenzó en el pasado y que es anterior a otra acción también en el pasado (el pasado simple).
explanation.en: We use the past perfect to refer to an event that began in the past and before another action or event in the past. The action or event that that happened first is in the past perfect and the one that follows in the past simple.

questions.text: They _____ never _____ (see) a 3D movie before 2009 when they _____ (see) "Avatar" for the first time.

explanation.es: Usamos el pasado perfecto para referirnos a una acción o evento que comenzó en el pasado y que es anterior a otra acción también en el pasado. La acción que ocurrió primero es en pasado perfecto y la que sigue en pasado simple.
explanation.en: We use the past perfect to refer to an event that began in the past and before another action or event in the past. The action or event that that happened first is in the past perfect and the one that follows in the past simple.

questions.text: She _____ (enjoy) the beach when it _____ (start) to rain.

explanation.es: Usamos el pasado perfecto continuo para acciones en proceso de realización en el pasado antes de otra acción en el pasado (el pasado simple).
explanation.en: We use the past perfect continuous for actions or events that were in process in the past, before another action or event occurred. The past perfect continuous is used for the action in process and the past simple for the action that interrupted it.

questions.text: He _____ (be) healthy all his life before he _____ (have) the heart attack.

explanation.es: También, como el presente perfecto, con algunos verbos usamos el pasado perfecto para situaciones que empezaron en el pasado y que siguieron hasta un punto específico en el pasado.  Hay unos verbos que no usamos en tiempos continuos.
explanation.en: As with the present perfect, we also use the past perfect simple for situations that began in the past and continued to a specific point in time in the past, as we cannot use the continuous tenses with certain verbs.

questions.text: I _____ (be) exhausted because I _____ (sleep, not) well.

explanation.es: Se utiliza el pasado perfecto continuo para demostrar causa y efecto en el pasado.
explanation.en: The past perfect continuous is used to show cause and effect in the past.

answer.raw: was, had not been sleeping | was, hadn't been sleeping

questions.text: They _____ never _____ (own) a house before they _____ (buy) the house next door.

explanation.es: También, como el presente perfecto, con algunos verbos usamos el pasado perfecto para situaciones que empezaron en el pasado y que siguieron hasta un punto específico en el pasado.  Hay unos verbos que no usamos en tiempos continuos.
explanation.en: As with the present perfect, we also use the past perfect simple for situations that began in the past and continued to a specific point in time in the past, as we cannot use the continuous tenses with certain verbs.

questions.text: Bill said that he ____ (run) in three marathons last year.

explanation.es: Se utiliza el pasado perfecto con el estilo indirecto.
explanation.en: We use the past perfect in reported speech.

questions.text: _____ you ever _____ (be) to a big city before you _____ (go) to New York?

explanation.es: Usamos el pasado perfecto para referirnos a una acción o evento que comenzó en el pasado y que es anterior a otra acción también en el pasado (el pasado simple).
explanation.en: We use the past perfect to refer to an event that began in the past and before another action or event in the past. The action or event that that happened first is in the past perfect and the one that follows in the past simple.

questions.text: Andy told us he ____ (mean) to call us for weeks.

explanation.es: Se utiliza el pasado perfecto con el estilo indirecto.  En este ejemplo la preposición de tiempo "for" indica el uso del pasado perfecto continuo.
explanation.en: We use the past perfect with reported speech. In this example, the preposition of time "for" indicates the use of the past perfect continuous.

instruction.es: Completa las frases con la forma correcta del verbo que se encuentra entre paréntesis.
instruction.en: Complete the sentences with the correct form of the verb in parenthesis.

questions.text: If I win the lottery, I ____ (buy) a new car.

explanation.es: Se utiliza el first conditional para el futuro.  Cuando la condición es en el presente simple, se usa "will" con el otro verbo
explanation.en: We use the first conditional for expressing the future and in cases when the condition is very likely to occur. When the condition is in the present simple, we use "will" with the other verb.

questions.text: If we spoke better English, we ____ (move) to the United States.

explanation.es: Se utiliza el tipo 2 para expresar una posibilidad irreal en el presente, como un deseo o un sueño, o para una acción en el futuro no muy probable. En este tipo de condicional, se utiliza el pasado simple y el auxiliar "would" + infinitivo.
explanation.en: The second conditional is used to express an unreal possibility in the present, such as a wish or dream, or for an action in the future that is not very probable. In the second conditional, the condition is in the past simple and we use "would".

questions.text: If it ____ (rain), we should go to the movies.

explanation.es: Se utiliza el tipo 1 del condicional para el futuro. Podemos usar algunos verbos modales (como "should" o "could) en vez de "will" para cambiar la probabilidad o expresar una opinión.
explanation.en: We use the first conditional for expressing the future and in cases when the condition is very likely to occur. We can use certain modal verbs (such as "should" or "could) instead of “will” to change the probability of the condition or express an opinion.

questions.text: They ____ (wait, not) in the rain, had you called them to tell them you were going to be late.

explanation.es: A diferencia de los tipos 1 y 2, se utiliza el tercer tipo del condicional cuando hablamos de una condición en el pasado que no ha sucedido. En el tipo 3, se utiliza el pasado perfecto y "would have" + el participio pasado.
explanation.en: As opposed to the first and second conditionals, the third conditional is used to speak about a condition in the past that has not occurred, therefore the condition is in the past perfect.

answer.raw: would not have waited | wouldn't have waited

questions.text: If Peter ____ (work) less, he would have more time to study.

explanation.es: Se utiliza el tipo 2 para expresar una posibilidad irreal en el presente, como puede ser un deseo o un sueño, o para una acción en el futuro no muy probable. En este tipo de condicional, se utiliza el pasado simple y el auxiliar "would" + infinitivo.
explanation.en: The second conditional is used to express an unreal possibility in the present, such as a wish or dream, or for an action in the future that is not very probable. With the second conditional, we use the past simple and "would".

questions.text: It would have been a nice holiday, if I ____ (break, not) my leg.

explanation.es: A diferencia de los tipos 1 y 2, se utiliza el tercer tipo del condicional cuando hablamos de una condición en el pasado que no ha sucedido. En el tipo 3, se utiliza el pasado perfecto y "would have" + el participio pasado.
explanation.en: As opposed to the first and second conditionals, the third conditional is used to speak about a condition in the past that has not occurred, we therefore use the past perfect.

questions.text: _____ you _____ (go) to a different show if you can't get tickets to "Les Miserables"?

explanation.es: Se utiliza el tipo 1 del condicional para el futuro.  Cuando la condición se da en el presente simple, se usa "will" con el otro verbo.
explanation.en: We use the first conditional for expressing the future and in cases when the condition is very likely to occur. When the condition is in the present simple, we use "will" with the other verb.

questions.text: If she were shorter, she ____ (wear) high heels more often.

explanation.es: Se utiliza el tipo 2 para expresar una posibilidad irreal en el presente, como puede ser un deseo o un sueño, o para una acción en el futuro no muy probable. En este tipo de condicional, se utiliza el pasado simple y el auxiliar "would" + infinitivo.
explanation.en: The second conditional is used to express an unreal possibility in the present, such as a wish or dream, or for an action in the future that is not very probable. With this type of conditional, we use the past simple and the auxiliary "would".

questions.text: _____ you _____ (be) angry if I hadn't called to tell you I would be late?

explanation.es: A diferencia de los tipos 1 y 2, se utiliza el tercer tipo del condicional cuando hablamos de una condición en el pasado que no ha sucedido. En el tipo 3, se utiliza el pasado perfecto y "would have" + el participio pasado.
explanation.en: As opposed to the first and second conditionals, the third conditional is used to speak about a condition in the past that has not occurred, we therefore use the past perfect.

instruction.es: Escribe las siguientes frases en la voz pasiva.
instruction.en: Write the following sentences in the passive voice.

questions.text: Rewrite the sentence in the passive voice: Mark Twain wrote "The Adventures of Tom Sawyer" in 1876.

explanation.es: Se forma la voz pasiva con el verbo auxiliar "to be" y el participio pasado del verbo.  El verbo principal se sustituye por el auxiliar "to be", en su mismo tiempo, junto al verbo principal en participio.
explanation.en: The passive is formed using the auxiliary verb “to be” and the past participle of the principal verb. If in the passive we mention the subject who completed the action, this subject is normally introduced with the preposition "by".

answer.raw: "The Adventures of Tom Sawyer" was written by Mark Twain in 1876. | "The Adventures of Tom Sawyer" was written in 1876 by Mark Twain.
accepted: "The Adventures of Tom Sawyer" was written by Mark Twain in 1876.
"The Adventures of Tom Sawyer" was written in 1876 by Mark Twain.
accepted_placeholders: "The Adventures of Tom Sawyer" was written by Mark Twain in 1876.
"The Adventures of Tom Sawyer" was written in 1876 by Mark Twain.

questions.text: Rewrite the sentence in the passive voice: They make many movies in Hollywood.

explanation.es: Se forma la voz pasiva con el verbo auxiliar "to be" y el participio pasado del verbo.  El verbo principal se sustituye por el auxiliar "to be", en su mismo tiempo, junto al verbo principal en participio.
explanation.en: The passive is formed using the auxiliary verb “to be” and the past participle of the principal verb. The principal verb in the active voice is substituted by the auxiliary verb “to be”, in the same verb tense, and the verb is then in the past participle.

answer.raw: Many movies are made in Hollywood.
accepted: Many movies are made in Hollywood.
accepted_placeholders: Many movies are made in Hollywood.

questions.text: Rewrite the sentence in the passive voice: The students will give a presentation tomorrow.

explanation.es: Se forma la voz pasiva con el verbo auxiliar "to be" y el participio pasado del verbo.  El verbo principal se sustituye por el auxiliar "to be", en su mismo tiempo, junto al verbo principal en participio.
explanation.en: The passive is formed using the auxiliary verb “to be” and the past participle of the principal verb. The principal verb in the active voice is substituted by the auxiliary verb “to be”, in the same verb tense, and the verb is then in the past participle.

answer.raw: A presentation will be given tomorrow by the students. | A presentation will be given by the students tomorrow.
accepted: A presentation will be given tomorrow by the students.
A presentation will be given by the students tomorrow.
accepted_placeholders: A presentation will be given tomorrow by the students.
A presentation will be given by the students tomorrow.

questions.text: Rewrite the sentence in the passive voice: Someone has stolen my bike!

explanation.es: Se forma la voz pasiva con el verbo auxiliar "to be" y el participio pasado del verbo.  No hace falta la preposición "by" en este ejemplo porque no sabemos quien realiza la acción.
explanation.en: The passive is formed using the auxiliary verb “to be” and the past participle of the principal verb. The preposition "by" is not necessary in this example because we don't know who carried out the action.

questions.text: Rewrite the sentence in the passive voice: We receive the newspaper everyday.

explanation.es: Se forma la voz pasiva con el verbo auxiliar "to be" y el participio pasado del verbo.  El verbo principal se sustituye por el auxiliar "to be", en su mismo tiempo, junto al verbo principal en participio.
explanation.en: The passive is formed using the auxiliary verb “to be” and the past participle of the principal verb. The principal verb in the active voice is substituted by the auxiliary verb “to be”, in the same verb tense, and the verb is then in the past participle.

questions.text: Rewrite the sentence in the passive voice: The children have eaten the cookies.

explanation.es: Se forma la voz pasiva con el verbo auxiliar "to be" y el participio pasado del verbo. Si hacemos mención en la oración al sujeto que realiza la acción, éste irá precedido por la preposición "by".
explanation.en: The passive is formed using the auxiliary verb “to be” and the past participle of the principal verb. If in the passive we mention the subject who completed the action, this subject is normally introduced with the preposition "by".

answer.raw: The cookies have been eaten by the children.
accepted: The cookies have been eaten by the children.
accepted_placeholders: The cookies have been eaten by the children.

explanation.en: The passive is formed using the auxiliary verb “to be” and the past participle of the principal verb. The principal verb in the active voice is substituted by the auxiliary verb “to be”, in the same verb tense, and the verb is then in the past participle.

explanation.en: The passive is formed using the auxiliary verb “to be” and the past participle of the principal verb. The principal verb in the active voice is substituted by the auxiliary verb “to be”, in the same verb tense, and the verb is then in the past participle.

instruction.es: Escribe las siguientes frases en estilo indirecto, como en el ejemplo (He said he was happy.).
instruction.en: Write the following sentences in reported speech, as in the example (He said he was happy.).

questions.text: Construct Reported Speech: I am happy.

explanation.es: Cuando el verbo en la frase original es en el presente simple, cambia al pasado simple en indirecto.
explanation.en: When the verb in the original sentence is in the present simple, it changes to the past simple in reported speech.

answer.raw: He said he was happy. | He said that he was happy.
accepted: He said that he was happy.
accepted_placeholders: He said that he was happy.

questions.text: Construct Reported Speech: I am sleeping.

explanation.es: Cuando el verbo en la frase original es en el presente continuo, cambia al pasado continuo en indirecto.
explanation.en: When the verb in the original sentence is in the present continuous, it changes to the past continuous in reported speech.

answer.raw: He said that he was sleeping. | He said he was sleeping.
accepted: He said that he was sleeping.
accepted_placeholders: He said that he was sleeping.

questions.text: Construct Reported Speech: I was happy.

explanation.es: Cuando el verbo en la frase original es en el pasado simple, cambia al pretérito pluscuamperfecto en indirecto.
explanation.en: When the verb in the original sentence is in the past simple, it changes to the past perfect simple in reported speech.

answer.raw: He said he had been happy. | He said that he had been happy. | He said he'd been happy. | He said that he'd been happy.
accepted: He said he had been happy.
He said that he had been happy.
He said that he'd been happy.
accepted_placeholders: He said he had been happy.
He said that he had been happy.
He said that he'd been happy.

questions.text: Construct Reported Speech: I have been happy.

explanation.es: Cuando el verbo en la frase original es en el presente perfecto simple, cambia al pretérito pluscuamperfecto en indirecto.
explanation.en: When the verb in the original sentence is in the present perfect simple, it changes to the past prefect simple in reported speech.

answer.raw: He said he'd been happy. | He said he had been happy. | He said that he had been happy. | He said that he'd been happy.
accepted: He said he had been happy.
He said that he had been happy.
He said that he'd been happy.
accepted_placeholders: He said he had been happy.
He said that he had been happy.
He said that he'd been happy.

questions.text: Construct Reported Speech: I have been sleeping.

explanation.es: Cuando el verbo en la frase original es en el presente perfecto continuo, cambia al pasado perfecto continuo en indirecto.
explanation.en: When the verb in the original sentence is in the present perfect continuous, it changes to the past perfect continuous in reported speech.

answer.raw: He said that he's been sleeping. | He said he had been sleeping. | He said that he had been sleeping. | He said he'd been sleeping.
accepted: He said that he's been sleeping.
He said he had been sleeping.
He said that he had been sleeping.
accepted_placeholders: He said that he's been sleeping.
He said he had been sleeping.
He said that he had been sleeping.

questions.text: Construct Reported Speech: I will have been happy.

explanation.es: Cuando el verbo en la frase original es un verbo modal, cambia a la forma pasada en el indirecto.
explanation.en: When the verb in the original sentence is a modal verb, it changes to the past tense form in reported speech.

answer.raw: He said he would've been happy. | He said he would have been happy. | He said that he would have been happy.  | He said that he would've been happy.
accepted: He said he would've been happy.
He said he would have been happy.
He said that he would have been happy.
He said that he would've been happy.
accepted_placeholders: He said he would've been happy.
He said he would have been happy.
He said that he would have been happy.
He said that he would've been happy.

instruction.es: Escribe las siguientes frases en estilo indirecto, como en el ejemplo (He said he was happy.).
instruction.en: Write the following sentences in reported speech, as in the example (He said he was happy.).

questions.text: "I'm hungry," Tom said.

explanation.es: En el estilo indirecto, el presente simple cambia al pasado simple.  El uso de "that" en este ejemplo es opcional.
explanation.en: In reported speech, the present simple becomes the past simple. The use of "that" in this example is optional.

answer.raw: Tom said that he was hungry. | Tom said he was hungry.
accepted: Tom said that he was hungry.
accepted_placeholders: Tom said that he was hungry.

questions.text: Sarah asked, "Are you busy tonight?

explanation.es: En el estilo indirecto, el presente simple cambia al pasado simple.  En este ejemplo, como con todas las preguntas, se necesita "if" o "whether".
explanation.en: In reported speech, the present simple becomes the past simple. In this example, as with all questions in reported speech, we must use "if" or "whether", whereas the us of the object pronoun is optional.

answer.raw: Sarah asked if I was busy that night. | Sarah asked me if I was busy that night. | Sarah asked whether I was busy that night. | Sarah asked me whether I was busy that night.
accepted: Sarah asked if I was busy that night.
Sarah asked me if I was busy that night.
Sarah asked whether I was busy that night.
Sarah asked me whether I was busy that night.
accepted_placeholders: Sarah asked if I was busy that night.
Sarah asked me if I was busy that night.
Sarah asked whether I was busy that night.
Sarah asked me whether I was busy that night.

questions.text: "I am working in a hospital," she said.

explanation.es: En el estilo indirecto, el presente continuo cambia al pasado continuo.  El uso de "that" en este ejemplo es opcional.
explanation.en: In reported speech, the present continuous becomes the past continuous. The use of "that" in this example is optional.

answer.raw: She said she was working in a hospital. | She said that she was working in a hospital.
accepted: She said she was working in a hospital.
She said that she was working in a hospital.
accepted_placeholders: She said she was working in a hospital.
She said that she was working in a hospital.

questions.text: "We made a great dinner last night," he said.

explanation.es: En el estilo indirecto, el pasado simple cambia al pretérito pluscuamperfecto.  El uso de "that" en este ejemplo es opcional.
explanation.en: In reported speech, the past simple becomes the past perfect simple. Expressions of time also change. The use of "that" in this example is optional.

answer.raw: He said they had made a great dinner the previous night. | He said they had made a great dinner the night before. | He said that they had made a great dinner the night before.  | He said that they had made a great dinner the previous night.
accepted: He said they had made a great dinner the previous night.
He said they had made a great dinner the night before.
He said that they had made a great dinner the night before.
He said that they had made a great dinner the previous night.
accepted_placeholders: He said they had made a great dinner the previous night.
He said they had made a great dinner the night before.
He said that they had made a great dinner the night before.
He said that they had made a great dinner the previous night.

questions.text: "They were living in Chicago when their son was born," she said.

explanation.es: En el estilo indirecto, el pasado continuo cambia al pretérito pluscuamperfecto continuo.  El uso de "that" en este ejemplo es opcional. Nota que el tiempo del verbo en la segundoa parte de la frase no cambia.
explanation.en: In reported speech, the past simple becomes the past perfect simple. The use of "that" in this example is optional. Note that the second verb in the sentence does not change.

answer.raw: She said they had been living in Chicago when their son was born. | She said that they had been living in Chicago when their son was born. | She said they'd been living in Chicago when their son was born.
accepted: She said they had been living in Chicago when their son was born.
She said that they had been living in Chicago when their son was born.
She said they'd been living in Chicago when their son was born.
accepted_placeholders: She said they had been living in Chicago when their son was born.
She said that they had been living in Chicago when their son was born.
She said they'd been living in Chicago when their son was born.

explanation.es: En el estilo indirecto, el presente perfecto cambia al pretérito pluscuamperfecto.  El uso de "that" en este ejemplo es opcional.
explanation.en: In reported speech, the present perfect simple becomes the past perfect simple. The use of "that" is optional.

answer.raw: Melissa said that she had been to India three times.| Melissa said she had been to India three times. | Melissa said she'd been to India three times. | Melissa said that she'd been to India three times.
accepted: Melissa said that she had been to India three times.
Melissa said she had been to India three times.

answer.raw: Pam said she is from Australia. | Pam said that she is from Australia. | Pam said she was from Australia. | Pam said that she was from Australia.

explanation.en: "Every" can be used with adverbs.

questions.text: Not _____ good idea can become a reality.

explanation.es: “Every” se puede utilizar con sustantivos abstractos.
explanation.en: “Every” can be used with abstract nouns, but not “each”.

questions.text: We _____ had a chance to play the game.

explanation.es: “Each”, a diferencia de “every”, puede utilizarse también como un pronombre indefinido.
explanation.en: “Each”, unlike “every”, can also be used as an indefinite pronoun.

questions.text: The children read 20 minutes _____ day.

explanation.es: "Every day" significa "cada día".
explanation.en: "Every day" is a common expression.

questions.text: _____ one of you needs to write a description.

explanation.es: “Each” se puede usar con la preposición “of”. En esta construcción, “each of” va seguida por un pronombre o un sustantivo con un determinante.
explanation.en: "Each" can be used with the preposition "of" and is followed by a pronoun or a noun with an article.

questions.text: _____ time I go shopping I buy more than I intended.

explanation.es: "Every time" es una expresión común y significa "cada vez".
explanation.en: "Every time" is a common expression.

questions.text: There are two boxes. _____ one has a reference number.

explanation.es: "Each" enfatiza cada uno individualmente o por separado.
explanation.en: "Each" emphasizes every one individually or separately.

questions.text: We used to go skiing _____ winter when we were kids.

explanation.es: "Every" se utiliza para expresar la frecuencia con que algo sucede o sucedía.
explanation.en: "Every" is used to express how often something occurs or occurred.

instruction.es: Completa las frases con la forma correcta de: "go", "come", "take", "bring".
instruction.en: Complete the sentences with the correct form of: go, come, take, bring

questions.text: Where are you ____ on holiday this year?
_____ it with you, but don't forget to _____ it back tomorrow.
Did John _____ to the party?
_____ back here right now! You are in big trouble!
Who are you _____ with you to the wedding?
Can you _____ me the salt from the kitchen, please?
Please _____ some cookies home with you. There are so many.
Why don't you _____ to my house after school?

instruction.es: Elige la respuesta correcta.
instruction.en: Choose the correct answer.

questions.text: Students, please _____ to my desk and I will give you your exams.

explanation.es: "Come" indica un movimiento hacia o en la dirección del orador o la persona a la que se habla.
explanation.en: "Come" indicates a movement towards or in the direction of the speaker or person being spoken to.

questions.text: I would like two hamburgers to _____ away.

explanation.es: "Take", tal como "go", indica un movimiento de alejamiento del sitio actual del orador o la persona a la que se habla.
explanation.en: "Take", like "go', indicates a movement away from the current location of the speaker or person being spoken to.

questions.text: We need to _____ a ruler and a compass to school tomorrow.

explanation.es: As with "come", we use "bring" to indicate a movement in the direction of the speaker or person being spoken to.
explanation.en: Usamos "bring" para indicar un movimiento en la dirección del orador o la persona a la que se habla.

questions.text: Isn't this restaurant fabulous? We need to _____ here with Mom and Dad.

explanation.es: "Come" indica un movimiento hacia o en la dirección del orador o la persona a la que se habla.
explanation.en: "Come" indicates a movement towards or in the direction of the speaker or person being spoken to.

questions.text: It's so nice to be here at your party.  I _____ a bottle of champagne to celebrate.

explanation.es: Usamos "bring" para indicar un movimiento en la dirección del orador o la persona a la que se habla.
explanation.en: As with "come", we use "bring" to indicate a movement in the direction of the speaker or person being spoken to.

questions.text: Why is Andrew at the doctor’s office?

explanation.es: Andrew: "Well, I'm here for two reasons. These last few days I have had a very sore throat.; Andrew: I've been having a lot of pain in my left knee lately."
explanation.en: Andrew: "Well, I'm here for two reasons. These last few days I have had a very sore throat.; Andrew: I've been having a lot of pain in my left knee lately."

options.text: He has a cold and a sore foot.
He has an eye infection and a sore knee.
He has a sore throat and a sore knee.

questions.text: What does “I can hardly swallow” mean?

explanation.es: Andrew: "Well, I'm here for two reasons. These last few days I have had a very sore throat. I can hardly swallow..."
explanation.en: Andrew: "Well, I'm here for two reasons. These last few days I have had a very sore throat. I can hardly swallow..."

options.text: That he can't breathe through his nose.
That it's difficult for food, drink, etc. to go down his throat.

questions.text: What did the doctor prescribe?

explanation.es: Doctor: "It looks like a bad throat infection. I am going to prescribe antibiotics. You will have to take the pills for 7 days."
explanation.en: Doctor: "It looks like a bad throat infection. I am going to prescribe antibiotics. You will have to take the pills for 7 days."

questions.text: What does the doctor say is very important about taking antibiotics?

explanation.es: Doctor: "You should take one pill every 8 hours. Please don't forget to take them. Also you must finish the full 7-day treatment."
explanation.en: Doctor: "You should take one pill every 8 hours. Please don't forget to take them. Also you must finish the full 7-day treatment."

options.text: To take the pills after meals.
To not forget to take the pills.
To not forget any pills and finish the week long prescription.

questions.text: What has Andrew been doing lately?

explanation.es: Andrew: "Yes, I started running about 2 months ago. I have been running between 5 and 10 km a day."
explanation.en: Andrew: "Yes, I started running about 2 months ago. I have been running between 5 and 10 km a day."

questions.text: What did the doctor do to his knee?

explanation.es: Doctor: "Let me move your leg around and see...does this hurt?"
explanation.en: Doctor: "Let me move your leg around and see...does this hurt?"

options.text: She moved it in different positions.
She touched it in different places.

questions.text: What does he recommend Andrew do about his knee?

explanation.es: Doctor: "It looks like a ligament injury. You should rest your knee for about a week. Put ice pads on it a few times a day. And wear this knee brace when you walk."
explanation.en: Doctor: "It looks like a ligament injury. You should rest your knee for about a week. Put ice pads on it a few times a day. And wear this knee brace when you walk."

options.text: Rest his knee for a month and wear a knee brace.
Rest his knee for a week, put ice on it and wear a knee brace.

questions.text: When should Andrew return to the Doctor's office?

explanation.es: Doctor: "I wouldn't put too much pressure on your knee. Make an appointment for about 2 weeks from now and we'll see how it looks."
explanation.en: Doctor: "I wouldn't put too much pressure on your knee. Make an appointment for about 2 weeks from now and we'll see how it looks."

explanation.en: "Jessica and Lewis are a young couple and they are interested in buying their first home. They just got married two months ago..."

questions.text: John and Chris are shopping at a mall.

explanation.en: "John and Chris are at a great street market today."

questions.text: Chris is interested in a music record.

explanation.en: Chris:  "Wow! Here is the first album by U2. I would love to own this!"

questions.text: John asks the vendor to show him that the clock works properly.

explanation.es: John: "Does it work?";  Vendor: "Yes, of course. It just needs a new battery.";  John: "Do you have one so we can check?";  Vendor: "Sure, I'll show you."

questions.text: John bargains with the vendor and gets a good deal on the watch.

explanation.es: John: "How about $75?";  Vendor: "Sorry. I can't go lower than $100 I'm afraid.";  John: "Oh well, that's too bad. I can't afford it. Thank you either way."
explanation.en: John: "How about $75?";  Vendor: "Sorry. I can't go lower than $100 I'm afraid.";  John: "Oh well, that's too bad. I can't afford it. Thank you either way."
